## PAGE 01046: (2026x Refresh1) Link Throwing Intermediate Event

- page_id: `303998878`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998878/2026x+Refresh1+Link+Throwing+Intermediate+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:40.172+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Intermediate Throwing Event
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Link Throwing Intermediate Event is used to throw a link to a Link Catching Intermediate Event.

This event displays either of the following:

- A Link Throwing Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Target Link Event property value if the name is unspecified.

**Notation** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related element**

- Intermediate Throwing Event

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

1358373574

1358373575

1358373574

By now you should know how to write a basic template that makes a simple reference to an element. But how will you know if the element is referenced or not? This section shows you how elements are referenced and how to call them.

1358373573

## PAGE 01048: (2026x Refresh1) List

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

- The UML Class must have the additional stereotype XSDlist.
- Binding between this class and XSD:list must be provided.
- “itemsType” maps to UML TemplateArgument from Binding.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### List model example.

#### PANEL: List XML code sample


```text

```

```text
<xs:list >
```

```text
<xs:simpleType>
```

```text

```

```text
</xs:list>
```

```text
</xs:simpleType>
```

```text
<xs:simpleType name="my_number_list" >		<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >my list documentation</xs:documentation>		</xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:list itemType="xs:boolean" />
```

```text
</xs:simpleType>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998150 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01049: (2026x Refresh1) List tool

- page_id: `303998150`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998150/2026x+Refresh1+List+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:02.762+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050360272

2050360275

2050360269

**On this page**

43

2050360274

$list is a list tool used to work with lists and arrays in a template. It provides a method to get and manage specified elements. You can also use it as a means to perform some actions on a list or array such as:

- Check if it is empty.
- Check if it contains certain elements.

For example:

| $primes$list.size($primes)$list.get($primes, 2)$list.set($primes, 2, 1)$list.get($primes, 2)For example:$list.isEmpty($primes)$list.contains($primes, 7) | -> new int[] {2, 3, 5, 7}-> 4-> 5-> (primes [2] becomes 1)-> 1-> false-> true |
| --- | --- |

###### **$list.contains(list, element)**

Check if a list or array contains certain elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list or array instance. |
| element | - | An element instance. |  |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if a list or array contains certain elements. Otherwise, return false. |

###### **$list.get(list, index)**

Return a specified element of a list or array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list or array instance. |
| index | - | An index of an object in a list. |  |
| Return | - | object | Return an object in a list. |

###### **$list.isArray(object)**

Check if an object is an array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | object | - | An object |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if the object is an array. Otherwise, returns false. |

###### **$list.isEmpty(list)**

Check if a list or array is empty.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if the list or array is empty. Otherwise, returns false. |

###### **$list.isList(object)**

Check if an object is a list.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | object | - | An object |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true, if the object is a List. Otherwise, return false. |

###### **$list.set(list, index, value)**

Set a specified element of a List/array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects. |
| index | - | An index of an object in the list. |  |
| value | - | An object to be set to the list. |  |
| Return | - | object | An object at the previously-specified position with the set value. |

###### **$list.size(list)**

Return the size of a List or array.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | - | A list of objects. |
| Return | - | Integer | Return the size of the list as an Integer. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993766 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01050: (2026x Refresh1) Lock View tab

- page_id: `303993766`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993766/2026x+Refresh1+Lock+View+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:54.708+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface > (2026x Refresh1) Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Lock View tab is displayed only when working with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation' space='CM'].

The Lock View tab is used to display what was locked in the project (locked elements, project structure, symbol styles) and by which users. Next to the locked element, you can see the name of the user who has locked it. Using the **Filter by user**list in the Lock View tab toolbar, you can see the elements, locked by the particular user.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Lock View tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Lock View tab.
- If the Containment tab is hidden, from the Window menu, select Lock View .

The Locked Elements tab consists of the following two components:

- Lock View tab toolbar
- Lock View tree

##### Lock View tab toolbar

The Lock View tab toolbar contains the following:

- Update Lock Information button
- Filter by user list
- Collapse All button
- Collapse Selected Recursively button

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Using the **Filter by user** list you can select to display only the elements locked by the particular user.

##### Working with model elements in the Lock View tree

You can work with elements in the Lock View tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Lock | Locks or unlocks the particular element for edit. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Inheritance Tree | Opens the Inheritance tab and selects the current element in the Inheritance tree. |

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

only the elements locked by the particular user.</p><h3>Working with model elements in the Lock View tree</h3><p>You can work with elements in the Lock View tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:</p><table><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Lock</strong></p></td><td><p>Locks or unlocks the particular element for edit.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Select in Containment</strong> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the <br />current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Select in Inheritance</strong> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Inheritance tab and selects <br />the current element in the Inheritance tree.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="5571f32f-f404-4e11-944b-4b2f78980b29"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Model+Browser">Model Browser</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000219 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01051: (2026x Refresh1) Logging background actions

- page_id: `304000219`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000219/2026x+Refresh1+Logging+background+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:54.568+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The UAF Background actions Logging Feature logs and displays the hidden actions performed behind the scenes while working with non-symbolic diagrams, such as Gantt Charts, in the Enterprise model. The feature aims to bring more clarity by informing you about the additional elements and relationships created in the model but not directly visible in the diagram.

The logged background actions will be displayed in the notification window, allowing you to see what new elements and relationships are created and navigate to them in the Containment tree. The feature is available for various Unified Architecture Framework diagrams in the Strategic, Services, Resources, Personnel, Projects, and Standards domains. This implementation is supported across all Enterprise models, including DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0, and UAF.

For instance, in the Strategic Phasing diagram, after adding a new Capability Provision, you will be informed about the new Actual Project Milestones, Exhibits relationship, and Milestone Dependency Relationship created through the Capability Provision Creation Wizard. The user can navigate to these newly created elements and relationships from the notification window to the Containment tree.

Similarly, in the Personnel Forecast table, after filling in the relevant cells, the user will be informed about the newly created Forecast relationships for each Subject of Forecast, which they can navigate to in the Containment tree.

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Projects Roadmap diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Personnel Forecast diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Personnel Evolution diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Resources Forecast table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Standards Roadmap table' space='']

## PAGE 01052: (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint

- page_id: `303989958`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989958/2026x+Refresh1+Logical+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:18:45.607+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0
- labels: ['logical-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

821744036

821744038

821744037

The Logical viewpoint is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. It contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. The Logical viewpoint defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

821744035

**Views**

The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Logical viewpoint</span> defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56b7c146-9d0c-420a-8970-c7a1b0eb4431"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">821744035</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bedbd4c3-f4d4-4155-b224-684ef8680693" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998164 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01053: (2026x Refresh1) Loop tool

- page_id: `303998164`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998164/2026x+Refresh1+Loop+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:04.976+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

43

$LoopTool is used with the**#foreach** loops. It wraps a list to let you specify a condition to terminate the loop and reuse the same list in a different loop. The following example shows how to use **$LoopTool**.

```text
$loopTool.other
#if( $item >= 5 )$loopTool.stop()#end
#end

Output
------
1 -> 3
2 -> 4
3 -> 5
4 -> 6
5 -> 7]]>
```

###### **$LoopTool.wrap( list )**

Wrap a list with the tool.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | java.util.List | An array or a list instance. |
| Return | - | LoopTool | Return an LoopTool instance. |

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.hasMore()**

Check if the iteration has more elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | boolean | Return true if there are more elements in the wrapped list. |

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.more()**

Ask for the next element in the list.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.lang.Object | An element instance. |

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.remove()**

Remove the current element from the list.

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.reset()**

Reset the wrapper so that it will start over at the beginning of the list.

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.stop()**

Put a condition to break off a loop.

###### **$<LoopTool instance>.toString()**

Return an object as a string.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>$LoopTool is used with the<strong> #foreach</strong> loops. It wraps a list to let you specify a condition to terminate the loop and reuse the same list in a different loop. The following example shows how to use <strong>$LoopTool</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d622b74a-aa21-4514-9788-387f675e439c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set( $list = [1..7] )
#set( $others = [3..10] )
#foreach( $item in $loopTool.watch($list).sync($others, 'other') )
$item -> $loopTool.other
#if( $item >= 5 )$loopTool.stop()#end
#end

Output
------
1 -> 3
2 -> 4
3 -> 5
4 -> 6
5 -> 7]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><strong>$LoopTool.wrap( list )</strong></h4><p>Wrap a list with the tool.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>list </td><td>java.util.List </td><td>An array or a <br />list instance. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td>- </td><td>LoopTool </td><td>Return an <br />LoopTool <br />instance. </td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.hasMore()</strong></h4><p>Check if the iteration has more elements.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td>- </td><td>boolean </td><td>Return true if there <br />are more elements <br />in the wrapped list. </td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.more() </strong></h4><p>Ask for the next element in the list.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td>- </td><td> java.lang.Object </td><td>An element <br />instance. </td></tr></tbody></table><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.remove() </strong></h4><p>Remove the current element from the list.</p><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.reset() </strong></h4><p>Reset the wrapper so that it will start over at the beginning of the list.</p><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.stop()</strong></h4><p>Put a condition to break off a loop.</p><h4><strong>$&lt;LoopTool instance&gt;.toString()</strong></h4><p>Return an object as a string.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993515 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01054: (2026x Refresh1) Lr Lines of Development

- page_id: `303993515`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993515/2026x+Refresh1+Lr+Lines+of+Development
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:13.964+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['lr-lines-of-development', 'lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

67572108

67572110

67572109

**Description**

The Lr Lines of Development view specifies the logical threads (lines of development) for a set of projects and programmes. The status of each of these threads can be shown at the various milestones in the projects or programmes. The Lr view is primarily intended to support the acquisition process across multiple projects or programmes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

Use of the Lr view should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cr Capability Roadmap' space='']Capability Roadmapview.

The Lr view may be used for:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependencies and the identification of associated risk.
- Portfolio management.
- Through Life Management Planning (TLMP).

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix . [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_to_capability.png']
- Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_portfolio.png']

- Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart). [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_programme_to_capability_mapping.png']
- Lr Responsibility Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_matrix.png']

67572107

## PAGE 01055: (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following pages present detailed instructions on working with macros using Macro Engine. Macro Engine enables you to easily create, add, and edit your macros to customize them to your needs.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993737 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01056: (2026x Refresh1) Macro keyboard shortcuts

- page_id: `303993737`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993737/2026x+Refresh1+Macro+keyboard+shortcuts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:29.136+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Macro Engine allows you to assign keyboard shortcuts to the macros that you have created using either the **Environment Options** or **Macro Information** dialog, see figures below. You can later press the keyboard shortcuts to execute or run the macros.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

To open the keyboard shortcuts pane in the **Environment Options** dialog

1. Click Options > Environment on the main menu. The Environment Options dialog will open.
2. Click Keyboard, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The macro information and keyboard shortcuts that are saved either in the**Environment Options** or **Macro Information** dialog will be stored as a modeling tool's environment. They will not be kept in a specific project file [**.mdzip*].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the Macro Information or Environment Options dialog will be disabled until you type a keyboard shortcut in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box.

The table below lists the Keyboard Shortcut Buttons

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | To assign a new keyboard shortcut to a macro. |
|  | To remove the keyboard shortcuts from the selected item in box. |
|  | To remove all keyboard shortcuts from a macro. |

The table below lists the Keyboard Shortcut Text Boxes

| Field | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Current keys | To store a list of keyboard shortcuts currently assigned to a macro. |
|  | To type a new keyboard shortcut to be assigned to a macro. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01057: (2026x Refresh1) Main menu

- page_id: `303996576`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996576/2026x+Refresh1+Main+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:10.268+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface
- labels: ['file-menu', 'edit-menu', 'view-menu', 'layout-menu', 'diagrams-menu', 'options-menu', 'tools-menu', 'analyze-menu', 'collaborate-menu', 'window-menu', 'help-menu', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main menu located at the top of the modeling tool window contains the following elements:

| Menu element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| File | Contains basic project management-related commands, e.g., , , and . Among other actions, this menu also allows you to import data from external sources, export a project, or . |
| Edit | Use this menu when working with diagrams on the Diagram pane. The Edit menu contains the commands for selecting, symbols or entire diagrams, and finding elements in the current project. |
| View | Contains commands allowing you to of a diagram, use and customize a , manage diagram tabs (if you have several open diagrams) and of your modeling tool. |
| Layout | Use this menu for managing the of the symbols on an active diagram. To enable certain Layout menu commands, you must select more than one symbol. |
| Diagrams | Allows you to , access diagram wizards and history, and . |
| Options | Use this menu to specify and , select or , and choose the style for your modeling tool. |
| Tools | Contains the set of commands allowing you to execute for converting models between different modeling domains, , on the actual testing model, generate code for the selected model data, create and organize , access the Cameo DataHub Plugin, and more. |
| Analyze | Use this menu to access the, create implementation matrices, analyze and show model dependencies, , , or , and perform other model analysis-related actions. |
| Collaborate | Contains the commands for . |
| Window | Allows you to manage the layout of different windows in your modeling tool. |
| Help | Among other actions, this menu allows opening online documentation, managing resources, and accessing modeling tool-related information. |

#### NOTE: Customizable menus

Customizable menus

You can choose which of these menus are shown/hidden by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Perspectives' space=''].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Toolbars' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Image Library tab' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992123 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01058: (2026x Refresh1) Maintaining decomposed model integrity

- page_id: `303992123`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992123/2026x+Refresh1+Maintaining+decomposed+model+integrity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:35.042+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To maintain model integrity, there are two types of elements appearing in the model:

- Proxies
- Recovered elements

INLINE

A **proxy** is a lightweight surrogate that carries only the name and kind of the model element information. These proxies appear in the place of the original used project elements, and are dedicated to maintaining model integrity (so that there are no dangling ends of relationships, types of properties do not disappear, and so on), while the used project is not accessible (for example, you do not have permissions) or is not loaded.

When you do not have granted “Read model” permission for a used project, this used project will not be loaded in a project where it is being used. To maintain the model integrity and keep model references that point to elements of “not accessible” used project, proxies are being displayed instead of referenced not accessible elements. That means that you will be able to see only the following information of these elements:

- Metaclasses of the element
- Names of the element
- Owners of the element

Recovered element is really an indication of the dangling reference. Appearance of these elements indicates that there are elements in the project (for example, elements in the main project or its used projects) that refer to the element in the used project that was previously there but currently cannot be found. It may be that the element was unsafely deleted/ removed/somehow made unavailable in the module. In such case, a modeling tool continues maintaining model integrity of unresolved reference by recovering missing elements - so called “Recovered element” appear in a place of the missing element where the original element existed in the past.

Read how to manage recovered elements in the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To maintain model integrity, there are two types of elements appearing in the model:</p><ul><li>Proxies</li><li><span>Recovered elements</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7ee982-c055-4a89-a01d-0c05525a829b"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <strong>proxy</strong> is a lightweight surrogate that carries only the name and kind of the model element information. These proxies appear in the place of the original used project elements, and are dedicated to maintaining model integrity (so that there are no dangling ends of relationships, types of properties do not disappear, and so on), while the used project is not accessible (for example, you do not have permissions) or is not loaded.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>When you do not have granted “Read model” permission for a used project, this used project will not be loaded in a project where it is being used. To maintain the model integrity and keep model references that point to elements of “not accessible” used project, proxies are being displayed instead of referenced not accessible elements. That means that you will be able to see only the following information of these elements:</p><ul><li>Metaclasses of the element</li><li>Names of the element</li><li>Owners of the element</li></ul><p>Recovered element is really an indication of the dangling reference. Appearance of these elements indicates that there are elements in the project (for example, elements in the main project or its used projects) that refer to the element in the used project that was previously there but currently cannot be found. It may be that the element was unsafely deleted/ removed/somehow made unavailable in the module. In such case, a modeling tool continues maintaining model integrity of unresolved reference by recovering missing elements - so called “Recovered element” appear in a place of the missing element where the original element existed in the past.</p><p>Read how to manage recovered elements in the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="320d573d-cb09-4e14-9949-93db34336a46" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994575 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01059: (2026x Refresh1) Making the paths corners rounded

- page_id: `303994575`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994575/2026x+Refresh1+Making+the+paths+corners+rounded
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:24.723+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['path-corners-rounded', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To make the path corners rounded

1. Right-click the path and click Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Rounded Corners property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To make the path corners rounded</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the path and click <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set  the <strong>Rounded Corners</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995246 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01060: (2026x Refresh1) Making your own default style for new projects

- page_id: `303995246`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995246/2026x+Refresh1+Making+your+own+default+style+for+new+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:24.249+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2042041905

2042041917

2042041907

When you modify symbol styles in a projects, these styles will be available only in that project. However, you can make your own default symbol styles and use them for the new projects. In order to do that, you will be needing to modify the template.

To modify the template

1. From the <modeling tool installation directory>\templates , open the template.mdzip .
2. In the Project Options dialog, modify symbol styles.
3. Save the template.

You can create and define your own project and use it as template.

After you define your desired symbol styles, you can use them in your new projects. All you need to do is to create project from template.

To create a project from template

1. Do one of the following:
  - From the File menu, select New Project .
  - On the main toolbar, click the New Project button.
  - Press Ctrl + Shift + N.
2. Select the Project from Template in the Other domain.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Select your modified template.
5. Click OK .

2042041903

**Related Pages**

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997317 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01061: (2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites

- page_id: `303997317`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997317/2026x+Refresh1+Manage+favorites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:20.442+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Favorites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1542432437

1542432449

1542432439

The Manage Favorites dialog allows:

- Adding to favorites
- Removing from favorites
- Changing the order of favorites

To open the **Manage Favorites** dialog

- In the Model Browser, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] button (on the toolbar of the open tab), and then select Manage Favorites . [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_open_manage_fav_dlg.png']

The **Manage Favorites** dialog is a modification of the element Selection dialog. The dialog displays:

- All elements (in tree or list view on the left side of the dialog)
- Favorite elements (in the list on the right side of the dialog)
- Buttons for adding and removing from favorites
- Search modes and scope filters area
- Buttons for reordering favorites

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The order of favorites in the list of the **Manage Favorites** dialog meets the order of favorites on the Favorites menu. Thus, if you need to change the order of favorites on this menu, make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the **Manage Favorites** dialog. Buttons below the list will help you change the order of favorites.

1542432426

**Related pages**

- Favorites

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995215 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01062: (2026x Refresh1) Manage Navigation

- page_id: `303995215`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995215/2026x+Refresh1+Manage+Navigation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:23.667+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1761606203

1761606207

1761606206

You can define and manage navigation from the element. Here is what you can do:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog' space=''] .
- Set the active navigation target. You can choose which target should be opened when you double-click the element.
- Navigate to usages in owned diagrams and tables.
- View traceability, instances, etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing navigation

One element can have more than one target (hyperlink to web page, element, diagram, file, etc.) assigned, but you can choose which one should be set as an active target. The target which will be set as active is the one that opens when you double-click the element.

If the element has an active navigation target then an active navigation target icon is shown on a symbol. You can choose whether to show the icon

- by selecting the **Show Active Navigation Icons** command from the diagram toolbar Options. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- by changing a value of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space=''] **Show Active Navigation Icons**. By default, this option is set to *true*.

In the **Navigation/Hyperlinks** property group, you will find groups of suggestions for the active navigation target. You can choose from the suggestions of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks' space='']. The Default Navigation property means, that active target is the one defined by our rules. For example, for the Block the SysML v1 Internal Block Diagram will be opened.

1761606202

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

## PAGE 01063: (2026x Refresh1) Managing Block Interfaces

- page_id: `303993036`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993036/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+Block+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:43.873+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x Refresh1) Defining Interface Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All owned and inherited ports and their [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] of the selected [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] are collected on the left of the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] > **Ports/Interfaces**. Manage them by creating, redefining or deleting.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Direction | Direction prefix of the port. The tilde symbol (~) appears before the direction prefix when the port is conjugated. >]]> |
| Port Name | Name of the port. |
| Port Type | Type of the port. |
| Type Features | Features of the port type. |

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Opens the Specification window of the selected port. |
| Create | Opens the list with the available to create properties. Click to create the , , , , , . |
| Redefine | Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order. |
| Delete | Removes the selected item from the list. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000694 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01064: (2026x Refresh1) Managing Cr diagram

- page_id: `304000694`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000694/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:34.228+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in CV-3 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Capabilities

To add a new Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done

##### Adding Sub Capabilities

To add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

To add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Capability Provisions

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the Cr diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

The capability name becomes [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Gantt chart properties' space=''] when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Removing Capabilities

To remove a Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Removing Sub Capabilities

To remove a Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/></ac:link>.</strong></span></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Displaying all Sub Capabilities</h3><p>To display all Sub Capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong><strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><h3>Removing Sub Capabilities</h3><p>To remove a Sub Capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> Remove From Chart</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Sub Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong><strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><h3>Editing Capability Provision</h3><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capability Provision</h3><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991385 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01065: (2026x Refresh1) Managing CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `303991385`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991385/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:41.449+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Capability viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in CV-3 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Capabilities

To add a new Capability

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done

##### Adding Sub Capabilities

To add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

To add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Capability Provisions

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the CV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

The capability name becomes [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Gantt chart properties' space=''] when one or more sub capabilities are deleted from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Deleting Capabilities and Sub Capabilities

To remove a Capability or Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability or Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability or Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability or Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990102 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01066: (2026x Refresh1) Managing derived SysML properties and tags

- page_id: `303990102`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990102/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+derived+SysML+properties+and+tags
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:15.556+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Functional analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Functional Allocations
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All derived SysML properties and tags are collected in the **Allocations** property group.

To add or remove an element

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] on the left.
2. Select an element from the Select Elements dialog.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_element_button.png'] to add element / [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_element_button.png'] to remove element.
4. Click OK .

The corresponding relationships are created and collected in the **Relations**property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Allocated From | Elements that are allocated to this element. |
| Allocated To | Elements the selected element is allocated to. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>All derived SysML properties and tags are collected in the <strong>Allocations</strong> property group.</p><p>To add or remove an element</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the left.</li><li>Select an element from the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Add" ac:alt="Add"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to add element /<ac:image ac:title="Remove" ac:alt="Remove"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to remove element.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The corresponding relationships are created and collected in the <strong>Relations </strong>property group.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Allocations property group" ac:alt="Allocations property group"><ri:attachment ri:filename="allocation_property_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing derived SysML properties and tags" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Column name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Allocated From</strong></td><td><p>Elements that are allocated to this element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Allocated To</strong></td><td><p>Elements the selected element is allocated to.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992425 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01067: (2026x Refresh1) Managing element groups

- page_id: `303992425`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992425/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+element+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:56.461+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create element groups to more easily manage selected (possibly heterogeneous) model elements. For example, you can group elements associated with a particular model release, elements that have a certain risk level, or requirements that need to be satisfied. New elements can be added to a group and unnecessary ones removed from it any time directly from the element shortcut menu.

To create an element group

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an element.
2. Click Element Group > Create Group . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_element_group.png']
3. Name the newly created group and specify other properties. [ATTACHMENT filename='element_group_specification_window.png']
4. Click Close when you are done. The element group is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='created_element_group.png']

To add an element to an element group

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an element and then go to Element Group .
2. Check the box next to the group you want to add an element to. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_element_to_group.png']

To delete an element from an element group

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an element.
2. Uncheck the box next to the group you want to remove an element from. [ATTACHMENT filename='removing_element_from_group.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create element groups to more easily manage selected (possibly heterogeneous) model elements. For example, you can group elements associated with a particular model release, elements that have a certain risk level, or requirements that need to be satisfied. New elements can be added to a group and unnecessary ones removed from it any time directly from the element shortcut menu.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element group</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click an element.</li><li>Click <strong>Element Group</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Group</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_element_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Name the newly created group and specify other properties.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_group_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> when you are done. The element group is created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="created_element_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an element to an element group</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click an element and then go to <strong>Element Group</strong>.</li><li>Check the box next to the group you want to add an element to.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_element_to_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To delete an element from an element group</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click an element.</li><li>Uncheck the box next to the group you want to remove an element from. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_element_from_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing element groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995043 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01068: (2026x Refresh1) Managing Excel and CSV file

- page_id: `303995043`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995043/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:01.685+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using UML properties upon importUsing UML properties upon import

If you want to use UML properties when importing a file, you can do by updating the system properties file.

To use UML properties upon import

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -DUSE_UML_TYPES_IN_EXCELSYNC=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import

By default, the import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.

- If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).

To skip empty rows/columns via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the numberEmptyRowsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.
5. Select the numberEmptyColumnsSkip tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
6. On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.
7. Click **Close**.

To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the **JAVA_ARGS** line, add the following values, and replace *POSITIVE_NUMBER* with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### To export by IDMapping elements by ID upon export

By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.

If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.

To map elements by ID via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **exportByIdentificationProperty** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To map elements by ID via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

##### Color marking modified data upon export

If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.

- If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.
- To apply color marking, mapping elements by ID must be enabled first.
- Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon Read From File action.

To color mark modified data via table tag

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. In the property groups list, select Tags . If you cannot see the Tags group, make sure the Properties mode is set to All .
3. Select the **markExportedRows** tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the **Create Value** button.
4. On the right side of the window, click the **Edit** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to set the tag value to *true*.
5. Click **Close**.

To color mark modified data via the system properties file

1. Go to the <modeling_tool_installation_dir>\bin\ and open the <modeling_tool>.properties file for editing.
2. In the file, find the JAVA_ARGS line and add -Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true as one of the values.
3. Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.

As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:

- Red - removed
- Blue - updated
- Green - added

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table

To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Open File . The linked file is opened.

##### Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table

If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created.

To remove it, follow the procedure below.

To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] .
2. Select Excel/CSV File > Clear Selection . The reference between tables is removed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3ca1be4-4bfd-4ac4-a218-4d3e42aeac48"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Using UML properties upon import</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Using UML properties upon import</h3><p>If you want to use UML properties when importing a file, you can do by updating the system properties file.</p><p><br />To use UML properties upon import</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9e67b198-1f6e-483e-b76b-9e0cc1b2ced5">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="c940a86b-93db-4c53-a0a1-c7fb8c5f5d21">In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-DUSE_UML_TYPES_IN_EXCELSYNC=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li data-uuid="8c073352-213c-4b92-80ba-b3a8f73a7c51">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3>Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import</h3><p>By default, the import of the Excel sheet's data is stopped if an empty row or column is encountered. If your Excel sheet contains purposefully created empty rows or columns and you do not want the reading of the file to be stopped by them, you need to specify that in the tool. You can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. By indicating the number of consecutive empty rows or columns that should be skipped, the file's data is continued to be read after the empty rows/columns.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b2d6e3a-8c64-4300-99b1-5479c4f00fe4"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="fbcfcb65-e491-4ffe-8aa5-c0c48687b3e3">If the options for skipping empty rows/columns are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different numeric values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li data-uuid="a8db3a98-4bc3-40c3-84ed-d6af23451440">The empty rows/columns themselves cannot be imported from the file to the modeling tool's table; they can either be treated as the end of the file (default) or skipped (see procedures below).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To skip empty rows/columns via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2875442d-f4d8-4a70-9a7e-faa622322b13">Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li data-uuid="bd308543-d771-45a7-8fd5-a62afe845d24">In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="26f9e7d6-7efd-4a49-a759-45ad10ec3a53">Select the <strong>numberEmptyRowsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="fb5656e2-d7ec-4842-827c-b8d4a4f986fc"><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty rows to be skipped.</span></li><li data-uuid="2b9852eb-1b48-4310-b7b2-fe9380af3f39">Select the <strong>numberEmptyColumnsSkip</strong><span> tag of the «MapToDataSource»  stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="afb64d4e-6981-4a81-b584-2e38bea445b2"><span>On the right side of the window, input a numeric value of consecutive empty columns to be skipped.</span></li><li data-uuid="525ef3b3-a0b0-4ba3-a0f3-5e179a9d82f6"><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To skip empty rows/columns via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5c65b9c3-e54d-423a-9862-ad9626d61354">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="4040fc6a-22cb-4f20-884e-befd59c2e5aa"><p>In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line, add the following values, and replace <span><em>POSITIVE_NUMBER</em> with a numeric value of consecutive empty rows/columns to be skipped:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8d1721b-4d65-424e-b438-1d455094bac5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Dnumber.empty.rows.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER
-Dnumber.empty.columns.skip=POSITIVE_NUMBER]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="bfa8e3ec-9b7d-45e3-a0a5-64a49d025bb6">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72ac8d2e-7190-4ed9-bbc8-ab71db7240b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">To export by ID</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Mapping elements by ID upon export</h3><p>By default, exporting table data to a synced file removes any previously existing content in the file. If you want to export table data to a synced file while keeping any existing data in the file, you can do so either by updating the system properties file or an appropriate table tag. This way, elements are mapped by ID, and the new data is added to the file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="998aa366-976e-4d34-8afa-5cf0b8c1d789"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If mapping options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To map elements by ID via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a5c8c6f4-73af-46ed-aed7-3ee8f3b08b54">Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li data-uuid="aab80719-93cf-4a96-84b8-d87d64dbb104">In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="cbc9274d-af6a-4ebe-9051-f40e6fd50ef3">Select the <span><strong>exportByIdentificationProperty</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button. </span></li><li data-uuid="23ad2fae-50a3-4479-9207-21d8817e301e"><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="0808ccc4-9fef-4ff8-b9ef-a5d9030121a7"><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To map elements by ID via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="bf5c5cd3-5e07-4342-ab54-4ec80c382e90">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="7f21d902-239d-4aa5-a4a5-322131ac008c">In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.by.identification.property=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li data-uuid="5e9b78db-0664-4a5b-ac0a-b0aab1fa7deb">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><h3>Color marking modified data upon export</h3><p>If you want to color-mark exported table data based on its changes (added/updated/removed, same as in the modeling tool's table), you can do so either by updating the system properties file or appropriate table tags. This allows you to easily review data changes and make any necessary adjustments based on this information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a294723e-2a8e-428f-a27b-dd6198b28341"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="1622fcb9-b35f-4dc2-a69c-c123aa4cc2c3">If color marking options are set both via the system properties file and the table tag, but they have different boolean values, the table tag value overrides the one set via the system properties file.</li><li data-uuid="d028258e-60d5-4838-80e4-a0322257ee0d">To apply color marking, <strong> mapping elements by ID </strong>must be enabled first.</li><li data-uuid="8ec20716-4b0f-4f25-93db-e7a28f92b8fc">Any data deleted in the table is marked in red in the exported Excel file. If, upon the file's review, you deem this data truly unnecessary, make sure to remove it from the Excel file. Otherwise, this data will be imported back into the table upon <strong>Read From File</strong> action. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To color mark modified data via table tag</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5b6edbb4-9b18-4a7f-bcd1-4ce3db91e3b1">Open the Specification window of the table.</li><li data-uuid="7d4e5435-2d9f-4772-bb47-9aec8a12fd52">In the property groups list, select <strong>Tags</strong>. If you cannot see the <strong>Tags</strong> group, make sure the <strong>Properties</strong> mode is set to <strong>All</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="3f55f3d4-b4be-43eb-b4ee-974d2b6dbed6">Select the <span><strong>markExportedRows</strong> tag of the «MapToDataSource» stereotype and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="c8768116-b897-48e6-9c21-7f46df929cd9"><span>On the right side of the window, click the <strong>Edit</strong> <ac:image ac:alt="edit_button.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/106627254/edit_button.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1644244174567&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button to set the tag value to <em>true</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="896d86d9-e319-4932-8a05-859b03092dca"><span>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To color mark modified data via the system properties file </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="50b362bf-5212-4647-9761-6e6e2a2e71a9">Go to the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_installation_dir&gt;\bin\</em> and open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool&gt;.properties</em> file for editing.</li><li data-uuid="3f9520b9-d16f-4f26-8adc-639ab002ef08">In the file, find the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line and add <em>-Dexport.table.mark.exported.rows=true</em> as one of the values.</li><li data-uuid="5f6e6418-44db-4de0-b928-157d6097432e">Save the file and re-open the modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br />As a result, the cells of the exported table are marked based on their changes in comparison to the data stored in the file prior to its export. The colors used for the marking and their meanings:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8d18af91-3ffe-4bd8-80bc-5b4225b78eac">Red - removed</li><li data-uuid="08d389bb-4bda-4767-a155-66687a94045a">Blue - updated</li><li data-uuid="406d57b0-ebb6-4491-a19a-52636c925025">Green - added</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Color_marking_exported_data.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><span>Opening linked Excel/CSV file with the table</span></h3><p>To open a linked Excel and/or CSV file directly from a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a6cc169d-0ec6-4345-b0ef-8ae3aa23f12a">On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="bcdb8c3d-3592-4c21-bdd0-2c4ad71dc71c">Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open File</strong>.<br />The linked file is opened.</li></ol><h3>Removing the reference between the Excel/CSV file and the table</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you linked the Excel/CSV file with modeling tool table, the reference between them is created.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To remove it, follow the procedure below. </span></p><p>To remove a reference between a Excel/CSV file and a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d535d55e-e1bf-44d8-b3a0-89600fa98b04">On the table toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="4d5a3539-920d-46d7-b0d6-199b5e6d42ba">Select <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> &gt; <strong>Clear Selection</strong>.<br />The reference between tables is removed.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 01069: (2026x Refresh1) Managing Interfaces of the Proxy Port

- page_id: `303993045`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993045/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+Interfaces+of+the+Proxy+Port
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:44.357+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x Refresh1) Defining Interface Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The detailed information about Proxy Port interfaces is collected on the left of the Port [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] > **Interface Block Properties**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Interface Block properties group in the Specification window of the Proxy Port.

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | Compartment name. |
| Type | Compartment type. |
| Default Value | Use to set the value manually. |
| Owner | which is the owner of the selected. |

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Opens the of the compartment |
| Up | Move item to upper position in the list. The items are automatically renumbered after moving. |
| Down | Move item to lower position in the list. The items are automatically renumbered after moving. |
| Create | Opens the list with the available to create properties. Click to create the , or . |
| Redefine | Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order. |
| Delete | Removes the selected item from the list. |
