````html
<p>The detailed information about Proxy Port interfaces is collected on the left of the Port <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Interface Block Properties</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interface_block_properties_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Interfaces of the Proxy Port" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Interface Block properties group in the Specification window of the Proxy Port.</h6><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Column name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>Compartment name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td>Compartment type.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Default Value</strong></td><td>Use to set the value manually.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Owner</strong></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interface Block" /></ac:link> which is the owner of the selected<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Proxy Port]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Open Specification" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Open Specification" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Interfaces of the Proxy Port" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Opens the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> of the compartment</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Up</strong></td><td><p>Move item to upper position in the list. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999529 space=MT version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 01070: (2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups

- page_id: `303999529`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999529/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+Map+Groups
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:28.871+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the Map Groups is to simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>

It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.

The following figure demonstrates the *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group that contains *CarBreak Requirments*, *CarBreak Structure* and *Car Break Satisfy*Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements*Map Group in the **Manage Map Groups** dialog.

##### Creating Map Groups

To create a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups .
2. In the **Tree** tab of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog, select the *Excel/CSV imports*Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Specification window opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Name** property value, type the name for the new Map Group and click the **Close** button.

The map group is created and becomes visible in the **Select Map Group for Editing**drop-down list box of the **Manage Maps Groups** dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.

##### Deleting Map Groups

To delete the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups .
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. Click the **Delete**button. The Map Group is deleted.

##### Adding import maps to a Map Group

To add Import Maps to a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']to add only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add it recursively.

The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group

##### Removing import maps from a Map Group

To remove an Import Map from a Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The**Manage Maps Groups**dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to remove.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove only the selected Import Map or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to remove them recursively.

The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.

##### Reordering import maps in the Map Group

By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.

To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups . The **Manage Maps Groups** dialog opens.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Select Map Group for Editing option and select the Map Group.
3. In the **Map Groups** area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.
4. Click **Up** to move the selected Import Map up or click **Down** to move the Import Map down.

The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Saving an Import Map' space='']
- Excel and CSV Import dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of the Map Groups is to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">simplify the import of Import Maps that depend on each other. By using the Map Groups you avoid having to manually import multiple maps individually</span>. You can collect saved Import Maps in map Group(s) to upload them one by one automatically when importing the Map Group. Before importing data by using an existing Map Group, you need to create them first and then add saved Import Maps. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Saving an Import Map" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to save Import Map >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0371a7b-7feb-4cf2-b478-dcb3347cfefc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is useful to create a Map Group only if it contains at least two Import Maps.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following figure demonstrates the <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group that contains <em>CarBreak Requirments</em>, <em>CarBreak Structure</em> and <em>Car Break Satisfy </em>Import Maps. When importing this Map Group, the Import Maps are executed in the same order as specified in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_map_group_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>CarBreak Structure Satisfy Requirements </em>Map Group in the <strong>Manage Map Groups</strong> dialog.</h6><h3>Creating Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f2a810cd-3e3e-4af9-90c6-81b1f9c380c3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li data-uuid="a6fc0556-83bb-43bc-9283-f0fa4fb45eb4"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Tree</strong> tab of the <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog, select the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>Excel/CSV imports</em> </span>Package as the owner for the new Map Group.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="c4927c38-d351-43a0-9054-0c98e984610b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_map_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. T<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">he Specification window opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="map_group_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></li><li data-uuid="4a933019-cc48-4563-8ae4-3b2a98d2d2a2"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Name</strong> property value, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">type the name for the new Map Group and click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</span></span></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The map group is created and becomes visible in the <strong>Select Map Group for Editing </strong>drop-down list box of the <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog. Now you can add Import Maps to the newly created Map Group.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3>Deleting Map Groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To delete the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Map Group</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f60dcc58-12ac-4615-a1d7-29cd58d4ba27"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li data-uuid="8e25b4f4-59b1-4909-b783-b8b18dad65a5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="e1fbda9a-bf56-4412-b9d0-8466e682e536"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Delete </strong>button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Map Group is deleted.</span></li></ol><h3>Adding import maps to a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To add Import Maps to a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e13639d0-ce18-4f28-8876-fff3cb186c65"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. The <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li data-uuid="e83d5014-2844-4eda-b3c8-59fd8e9b3206"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="5773f120-3bd6-48a0-a1bb-6180f27ac66f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From the Tree or List tab, select the Import Map you want to add to the selected Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="c1bd9312-13d3-43a1-adfb-abcfec7d62c9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>to add only the selected Import Map or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_recursively_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add it recursively.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Import Map(s) are added to the Map Group</span></p><h3>Removing import maps from a Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>To remove an Import Map from a Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0acd57da-dd2a-4cc2-9a05-4ec6aa9b2e5e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. T<span>he </span><strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong><span> dialog opens.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="ddaa97a3-1a49-4eb3-b943-95dfd4741655"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="76ea0454-86ed-4d07-ae6b-4734a43eb966"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Map Groups</strong> area, select the Import Map you want to remove.</span></li><li data-uuid="7f4e4e44-86ae-4d0c-8f27-0c118f215aae"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to remove only the selected Import Map or click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_all_import_map.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to remove them recursively.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Import Map(s) are removed from the Map Group.</span></p><h3>Reordering import maps in the Map Group</h3><p><br /></p><p>By specifying the order of the Import Maps in the Map Group, you define the order of their import.</p><p>To reorder the Import Maps in the Map Group</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="52092201-802b-45ae-9ccc-975bcbd59cc3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the </span><strong>File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Manage Map Groups</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. The <strong>Manage Maps Groups</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li data-uuid="3a1f5de5-5843-4e24-a643-cdbc4cf77787"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing Map Groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Select Map Group for Editing</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option and select the Map Group.</span></li><li data-uuid="383ab340-e0aa-4152-84c3-1f4a11bd06e0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Map Groups</strong> area, select the Import Map you want to reorder.</span></li><li data-uuid="a2e187ac-bf1c-4b77-8d37-026480f1ea8b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>Up</strong> to move the selected Import Map up or click <strong>Down</strong> to move the Import Map down.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Import Map(s) are reordered in the selected Map Group.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li data-uuid="12bff927-6f6e-4b5d-adac-f7e191178232"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog">Excel and CSV Import dialog</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000169 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01072: (2026x Refresh1) Managing project properties

- page_id: `304000169`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000169/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+project+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:52.867+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['architectural-description-element', 'root-element', 'manage-project-properties', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Architectural description as the root element" ac:title="Architectural description as the root element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="architectural_description.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing project properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:alt="Architectural Description Specification window" ac:title="Architectural Description Specification window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="architectural_description_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing project properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991561 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01073: (2026x Refresh1) Managing PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `303991561`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991561/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:49.655+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Project viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in PV-2 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Actual Projects

To add a new Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.

##### Adding Actual Sub Projects

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - Right click Actual Project and select Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created Actual Sub Project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Type its name
5. Specify Start Date and End Date for the Actual Sub Project.

##### Adding Actual Project Milestones

To add an Actual ProjectMilestone in PV-2 diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project **Milestone** .
  - right-click a Project and click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

##### editingEditing Project Status

To edit the Project Status in PV-2 diagram

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select **Edit Status**. If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] step 2 opens.
2. Edit the Project Status in the following way: 
a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area. 
b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.
3. Click **Finish** when you are done.

##### Relating Actual Projects

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate Actual Projects

1. Select two Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select the relation type:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between Actual Projects

1. Select two related Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. Right-click the highlighted relation and click Validation .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

##### Deleting Actual Project Milestones

To delete an Actual Project Milestone

- In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Delete .

##### Deleting Actual Projects

To delete a Actual Project from the chart

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Actual Project from the model

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### removeRemoving Project Status

To remove the Project Status

1. In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] step 2 opens.
2. In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can manage the data in PV-2 diagram in the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40c34fe0-78a7-4113-b30e-9b351e1978bb" /></p><h3>Adding Actual Projects</h3><p>To add a new Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Project</strong> dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.</li></ol><h3>Adding Actual Sub Projects</h3><p>To add a new Actual Sub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong> Actual Project</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Milestone</strong></span>.</li><li>right-click a Project and click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b587912c-1a77-409d-bb04-6b31cdbff20a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">editing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Editing Project Status</h3><p>To edit the Project Status in PV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09bbfbf5-0d29-4034-afb9-5987f3f65b10"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Edit Status</strong> and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> step 2 opens.</p></li><li><p>Edit the Project Status in the following way:<br />a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area.<br />b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.</p></li><li><p class="confluence-link">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><h3>Relating Actual Projects</h3><p>You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select the relation type:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> <br /></span></li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project sequence<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the highlighted relation and click <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li><p>Select one of the suggested solutions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Deleting Actual Project Milestones</h3><p>To delete an Actual Project Milestone</p><hr /><ul><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Deleting Actual Projects</h3><p>To delete a Actual Project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Actual Project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete From Chart</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Actual Project from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Actual Project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccc8194f-b1c8-4e01-8287-cd83bd4f1614"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Removing Project Status</h3><p>To remove the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> step 2 opens.</li><li>In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000289 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01075: (2026x Refresh1) Managing tables

- page_id: `304000289`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000289/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:58.109+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['table-toolbar', 'data-table', 'add-new', 'add-existing', 'delete-from-table', 'delete', 'default-owner', 'move-up', 'move-down', 'show-columns', 'report', 'show-full-types', 'show-rows', 'manage-tables', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216584849

216584851

216584850

The purpose of the tables is to improve usability of particular UAF products that cannot be achieved by using custom diagrams or matrices. Tables can be created from diagrams menu exactly the same as diagrams. All the tables in UAF plugin are editable.

The table pane consists of two major parts:

1. Toolbar . It's position is fixed at the top of the table.
2. Data Table. It is right below the toolbar.

These parts are arranged horizontally.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Toolbar buttons are quite similar for all tables. There are the **Add****New**, **Add****Existing**, **Delete****from****Table**, **Delete**, **Default****Owner**, **Move****Up** and **Move****Down**, **Show****Columns**, **Report**, **Export**, and **Show****Full****Types** buttons on the toolbar. Other buttons that are specific to particular product are described within the product specification sections.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add New | To create a new element in the model and a new row in the data table. |
| Add Existing | To allow you choosing one or more existing elements from the model and add a new rows to the data table. |
| Delete from Table | To remove the selected element and row from the data table. |
| Delete | To remove the selected element and row from the data table and the model. |
| Default Owner | To define Constrains and Measurement Sets default ownership. |
| Move Up | To move the selected row up. |
| Move Down | To move the selected row down. |
| Show Columns | To open the shortcut menu with a list of the available columns for the data table.You will find that currently displayed columns are selected.Click an unselected column to display it.Click a selected column to hide it. |
| Report | To create a corresponding spreadsheet report by passing all the elements form the data table directly to the report. The Report button is available for CV-5, OV-3 (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), OV-6a (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), SV-6, SV-7, SV-10a, SOV-4a, SvcV-7, and SvcV-10a, StV-5 tables. You can generate either an OpenDocument (.ods) or Microsoft Excel 2007 (.xslx) spreadsheet report. |
| Export | To export an active table either to a plain text file (.cvs) or to a Hypertext Markup Language (.html) format. |
| Show Full Types | To show or hide the full path (qualified name) of each element used in the data table. |

Tables are fully synchronized with model which means that modification to the table such as deleting or creating will affect model elements except for a few cases that are described within the product specification sections. 
 
 Some tables, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], allow rows filtering. 
 
 To filter row

1. Create an OV-3 or SV-6 table.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Show Rows button.
3. Deselect row types that you want to hide or select row types that you want to show in the table.

216584848

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tables' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992831 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01077: (2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references

- page_id: `303992831`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992831/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+unresolved+references
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:25.207+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning > (2026x Refresh1) Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1525838642

INLINE

1525838654

INLINE

1526101509

INLINE

**On this page**

3

1525838644

INLINE

An unresolved reference means that your project contains an element that refers to some used project element that has become inaccessible. This can happen because the project element is no longer shared, it was removed, it was moved to a non-shared location, etc. To maintain your model integrity and project composition when the element is inaccessible, the modeling tool recovers the inaccessible elements to maintain the references that are indicated in the project as unresolved.

##### Running composition integrity analysis

If recovered elements are detected, a summary of validation errors is shown in the status line. You can run the composition integrity analysis to find and examine the usages of the recovered elements in your project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A summary of validation errors in the status line.

To run the composition integrity analysis

1. Do either: The **Validation Results** panel showing the recovered elements usages in the project opens in a separate tab. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis In the **Active Validation Results** panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is REF or NRAPU. From its shortcut menu, select **Run Composition Integrity Analysis**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - composition integrity analysis in Model BrowserIn the Model Browser, right-click an el ement that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu, select Validation > Recovered Element > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='composition_integrity_analysis_from_model_browser.png']
  - On the diagram pane, select the element shape that refers to the recovered element. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_toolbar_recovered_elements_found.png'] > Run Composition Integrity Analysis . [ATTACHMENT filename='managing_recovered_element_from_smart_manipulator.png']

##### Identifying validation errors

These are the abbreviations of common validation errors and their explanations:

- REF : The element is a recovered element. This element is referenced by another element in the project.
- SRE : T he symbol usage of the recovered element. Diagrams may have more than one recovered element displayed as a symbol. Each symbol of a recovered element is listed as a SRE error.
- URE : The element referencing the recovered element.

##### Handling recovered elements

It is recommended that you eliminate recovered elements from your model, using one of the following methods:

- Eliminate the causes of the element recovery . This is the case when recovered elements still exist. Identify the cause of the element recovery and eliminate it by, for example, sharing the package that contains the recovered element, moving the element back from a non-shared location to the shared one, etc.
- Restore recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist but can be restored from the bits of information the modeling tool still has. The restored element has only ID, name, and type.
- Manage the unresolved references that refer to the appropriate recovered elements . This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist and cannot be restored.

The following links outline methods of managing unresolved references using specific commands:

1525838641

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation' space='']

1574919148

INLINE

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *[ATTACHMENT filename='Project Decomposition Description.mdzip']*****sample model that uses the following projects: *[ATTACHMENT filename='Analysis and Design.mdzip'],**[ATTACHMENT filename='Code Level.mdzip'],* and***[ATTACHMENT filename='domain.mdzip'] .*

Alternatively, you can open the model from*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\project decomposition .*

In the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="smart_manipulator_toolbar_recovered_elements_found.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <strong>Run Composition Integrity Analysis</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="managing_recovered_element_from_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p>The <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel showing the recovered elements usages in the project opens in a separate tab.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_results_panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Identifying validation errors</h3><p>These are the abbreviations of common validation errors and their explanations:</p><ul><li><strong>REF</strong>: The element is a recovered element. Identify the cause of the element recovery and eliminate it by, for example, sharing the package that contains the recovered element, moving the element back from a non-shared location to the shared one, etc.</li><li><strong>Restore recovered elements</strong>. This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist but can be restored from the bits of information the modeling tool still has. The restored element has only ID, name, and type.</li><li><strong>Manage the unresolved references that refer to the appropriate recovered elements</strong>. This is the case when recovered elements no longer exist and cannot be restored.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The following links outline methods of managing unresolved references using specific commands:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f337638d-9112-4a03-a02f-4096fcec762c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7bcd508a-c037-4b60-a337-4a6105d77277"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1525838641</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5f0c88fd-99de-47dd-b15f-185f160c696a">Related pages</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3384e73c-16b5-40d0-8cb8-e5b036cc6dd1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1574919148</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures on this page is the <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Project Decomposition Description.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></em><strong> </strong>sample model that uses the following projects: <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Analysis and Design.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>, </em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Code Level.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>,</em> and<em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="domain.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ri:attachment></ac:link> .</em></p><p>Alternatively, you can o<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">pen the model from </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\product features\project decomposition .</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992176 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01078: (2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects

- page_id: `303992176`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992176/2026x+Refresh1+Managing+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:40.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning
- labels: ['used-projects', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage usages of an open local project in the**Used Projects**dialog. To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='CM'].

To specify project usage options

1. Do one of the following: • From the Options menu, select Project Usages . • Right-click a used project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] and, from its shortcut menu, select Project Usages > Options . The Used Projects dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='used_projects_dialog.png']
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Specify the following project usage options: Dialog element nameDescription**Accessibility****Read-only**- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.**Read-write**- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.**Load Mode****Always load** (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.**Autoload** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.**Autoload with prompt** - this mode is similar to the **Autoload** mode, except that the modeling tool asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.**Manual load** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified. 
Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.**Use Index**Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.**Packages**this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.
  - Use the following commands: CommandDescription**Use Project**Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using other projects in a project' space='']** wizard opens.**Remove** > **Remove with References**Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.**Remove** > **Remove, keep references**Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:**Case #1**: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space=''].**Case #2**: You may need to refactor projects that are created with the modeling tool versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of the modeling tool have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.**Import**Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:Is not a MagicDraw native projectIs locked (valid for server projects only)Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.Used projects containing recovered elements cannot be imported. If you attempt to do so, an informational error message is displayed, halting the import. In this case, review and resolve the recovered element validation errors in the Validation Results panel. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references' space=''].**Reload**Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.**Unload**Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.**Open Project**Click to open the selected used project for editing.**Options**Click to open the **Project Options** dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.
3. Click**OK**.

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can manage usages of an open local project in the </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> dialog. To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p><p>To specify project usage options</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5dc3f5b1-b8b2-411f-9fa2-7a02e1a0b813">Do one of the following:<br />• From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong>.<br />• Right-click a used project in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and, from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Used Projects dialog" ac:alt="Used Projects dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_projects_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="45275fad-2c44-45ed-a86f-cea11139341a"><p>Do one or both of the following:</p><ul><li><p>Specify the following project usage options:</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Dialog element name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Accessibility</strong></td><td><ul><li><strong>Read-only </strong>- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.</li><li><strong>Read-write </strong>- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Load Mode</strong></td><td><ul><li><p><strong>Always load</strong> (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload with prompt</strong> - this mode is similar to the <strong>Autoload</strong> mode, except that the modeling tool asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Manual load</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified.<br />Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Use Index</strong></td><td>Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Packages</strong></td><td>this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.</td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul><p><span> </span></p><ul><li><p>Use the following commands:</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Command</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Use Project</strong></td><td><p>Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The <strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong> wizard opens.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove with References</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove, keep references</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:</p><p><strong>Case #1</strong>: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.</p><p>In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[replacing the initially used project with another project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Case #2</strong>: You may need to refactor projects that are created with the modeling tool versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of the modeling tool have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Import</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:</p><ul><li>Is not a MagicDraw native project</li><li>Is locked (valid for server projects only)</li><li>Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)</li></ul><p>The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.</p><p>Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0e458b7-430e-4a88-86c5-24f894158870"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Used projects containing recovered elements cannot be imported. If you attempt to do so, an informational error message is displayed, halting the import. In this case, review and resolve the recovered element validation errors in the Validation Results panel. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing unresolved references" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Reload</strong></td><td><p>Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Unload</strong></td><td><p>Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.</p><p>This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Open Project</strong></td><td>Click to open the selected used project for editing.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td><p>Click to open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b6ee2f07-b356-4461-a196-33c4405446f3"><p><span>Click </span><span> </span><strong>OK</strong><span>.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f4647c59-65ba-4a55-ae28-170f4669199b" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990300 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01080: (2026x Refresh1) Manipulations in the relation map

- page_id: `303990300`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990300/2026x+Refresh1+Manipulations+in+the+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:21.522+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Analysis diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-suppress-expand', 'relation-map-zoom', 'relation-map-hide', 'relation-map-remove', 'relation-map-restore-refresh', 'relation-map-validate', 'relation-map-decompose', 'relation-map-create-element', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manipulate and analyze your relation map in the following ways:

- / branches
- Move /
- / / /
- / an element
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

suppressTo suppress branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='suppress_button.png'] near the Node.

expandTo expand branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] near the Node.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

move_structureTo move the relation map structure

- Click on an empty place in the Relation Map and drag it. The whole structure will be moved appropriately.

move_nodeTo move the selected Node

- Click a Node and drag it. The selected Node will be moved appropriately.

zoom_inTo zoom in the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.
- Click the Zoom In button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_in.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad +.

zoom_outTo zoom out the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.
- Click the Zoom Out button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_out.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad -.

fitTo fit in window

Do one of the following:

- Click the Fit in Window button [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_in_window.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+W.

zoom_1_1To zoom 1:1

- Click the Zoom 1:1 button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_1_1.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad /.

hideTo hide an existing element in the diagram

- Press Delete.

removeTo remove an existing element from the model

- Press Ctrl+D.

restoreTo restore the layout of the manually suppressed/expanded branches, moved, removed and hidden symbols

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the View toolbar. If the**Preserve manually suppressed/expanded branches and hidden elements** option in the **Environment Options** dialog > **Diagrams** > **Relation Map**, is selected, the layout of moved elements is restored.

invalidTo start the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation' space='']of the relation map

- Click the Validate Diagram button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar of that diagram, table, matrix, or map. The diagram is validated against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.

containment_treeTo select an element in the Containment tree

- Select the element and click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_in_containment_tree.png'] on the Navigation toolbar.
- On the selected element shortcut menu, click Select in Containment Tree .

decomposeTo decompose an element

1. Select an element.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected element shortcut menu, select Create Related Element .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_element_button.png'] or press Insert (or [ATTACHMENT filename='command-button.jpg'] + I).
3. Select element type and relationship. If selected element type exists in the model, select an appropriate element from the list to represent it in the relation map. >]]> 
The element is decomposed.

openTo open an existing element list for the unnamed element

1. Select unnamed element.
2. Do one of the following: - Click the left mouse button. - Press Space. An existing element list opens, if such elements already exist in the model.

same_typeTo create element of the same type

1. Type element name.
2. Press Ctrl+Enter while in the name edit mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:link ac:anchor="expand"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expand ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>branches</li><li>Move<ac:link ac:anchor="move_structure"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ the whole structure]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>/ <ac:link ac:anchor="move_node"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the selected Node]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ri:filename="relation_map_expand_suppress.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Manipulations in the relation map" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76c6a8f1-da26-4e09-8bc5-e5b4ba9da7a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">move_structure</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To move the relation map structure</p><hr /><ul><li>Click on an empty place in the Relation Map and drag it.<br />The whole structure will be moved appropriately.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ebe64415-e784-4134-8714-1af281161f81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">move_node</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To move the selected Node</p><hr /><ul><li>Click a Node and drag it.<br />The selected Node will be moved appropriately.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="001edc97-9d56-4a7f-b5a8-441b766369ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="">zoom_in</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To zoom in the relation map structure</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.</li><li>Click the Zoom In button <ac:image ac:title="Zoom In" ac:alt="Zoom In"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_in.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+NumPad +.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0228966-ba90-46c4-824d-7786aabedc04"><ac:parameter ac:name="">zoom_out</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To zoom out the relation map structure</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.</li><li>Click the Zoom Out button <ac:image ac:title="Zoom Out" ac:alt="Zoom Out"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_out.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+NumPad -.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e532f24-ebfe-4a76-9ca2-3588a3623f58"><ac:parameter ac:name="">fit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To fit in window</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click the Fit in Window button <ac:image ac:title=" Fit in Window" ac:alt=" Fit in Window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fit_in_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+W.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78bd009d-cbc8-4663-a821-38f293944c11"><ac:parameter ac:name="">zoom_1_1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To zoom 1:1</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Zoom 1:1 button <ac:image ac:title="Zoom 1:1"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_1_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Press Ctrl+NumPad /.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7c7644f-2853-4598-93c5-c1672beb8f69"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To hide an existing element in the diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>Press Delete.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70c90757-28da-4b8d-ba76-7799e2b5e01c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove an existing element from the model</p><hr /><ul><li>Press Ctrl+D.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d51d368-a785-477d-8df2-d2eb0a669529"><ac:parameter ac:name="">restore</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To restore the layout of the manually suppressed/expanded branches, moved, removed and hidden symbols</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the View toolbar.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="001a79c8-a148-42b4-88d1-8361f28777b5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the<strong> Preserve manually suppressed/expanded branches and hidden elements</strong> option in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog &gt; <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Relation Map</strong>, is selected, the layout of moved elements is restored.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41d38c8a-ccdb-4a9c-a4ce-e771f051e101"><ac:parameter ac:name="">invalid</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To start the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validation ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Validate Diagram button <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Validate Diagram" ac:alt="Validate Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> on the toolbar of that diagram, table, matrix, or map. The diagram is validated against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db975f0e-96b7-4a2f-bc7c-194149d5cec7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">containment_tree</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select an element in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the element and click <ac:image ac:title="Select an element in the Containment tree" ac:alt="Select an element in the Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the Navigation toolbar.</li><li>On the selected element shortcut menu, click<strong> Select in Containment Tree</strong>.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fba24b3-7c73-4698-a562-231e40bd460e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">decompose</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To decompose an element</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the selected element shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Related Element</strong>.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Create Related Element" ac:alt="Create Related Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or press Insert (or <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="command-button.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>+ I).</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select element type and relationship.  If selected element type exists in the model, select an appropriate element from the list to represent it in the relation map. <ac:link ac:anchor="open"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open an existing element list for the unnamed last element >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />The element is decomposed.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20193be0-2b01-422f-9587-a0ecd1639aab"><ac:parameter ac:name="">open</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To open an existing element list for the unnamed element</p><hr /><ol><li>Select unnamed element.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - Click the left mouse button.<br /> - Press Space.<br />An existing element list opens, if such elements already exist in the model.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6481eb5-ed02-4c78-a43f-0bef60502651"><ac:parameter ac:name="">same_type</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create element of the same type</p><hr /><ol><li>Type element name.</li><li>Press Ctrl+Enter while in the name edit mode.</li></ol><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996677 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01081: (2026x Refresh1) Manual numbering

- page_id: `303996677`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996677/2026x+Refresh1+Manual+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:14.693+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Generic numbering mechanism > (2026x Refresh1) Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['how-to-update-the-last-id', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Using the manual numbering, you can:

- Set the ID for one or more elements.
- Increase, decrease, or change a number for an element.
- Add a prefix for the element number.
- Change a numbering format.
- Update the last ID.

To number the element

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the element list on the right side of the dialog, select an element you want to number.
3. Click Create .

To change the element’s numbering symbol

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click the Edit button.
  - If you need to change just the last number of the element ID, click **Edit**. The **Edit ID** dialog opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] You can enter only the number, if the selected element’s numbering value is numerical. 
You can enter only a new literal symbol, if the selected element’s numbering value is a literal symbol.
  - If you need to change the whole element ID (including the prefix), click Set Custom ID . The Custom ID dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Custom_ID_dialog.png']
3. Enter a new numbering symbol.
4. Click **OK**.

To remove the element’s number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element from which you want to remove the number and click the Remove button.
3. You can select either to remove the number only from the selected element or to remove numbers from all elements owned by the selected elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Remove button. Only the selected element’s number is removed.
  - Click the Remove Recursively button. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To increase the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to increase.
3. Click the Increase button. The element number is increased by one.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To decrease the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to decrease.
3. Click the **Decrease** button. The element number is decreased by one. The **Decrease** button is available, when the selected element’s number is not the first in the list.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To renumber the elements

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click Details to extent the dialog with the renumbering commands.
3. You can select either to renumber only elements listed in the element list or to renumber elements listed in the element list including their owning elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Renumber** button. All the elements in the elements list are renumbered successively.
  - Click the **Renumber Recursively** button. All the elements and elements owned by the selected element are renumbered successively.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

If the **Renumber from Initial Value** is selected, the whole element number changes. Otherwise just the number of the selected level changes.

To add a prefix to the element number

1. On the element owner’s shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Prefix column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the prefix. It can be any symbol.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates how numbers with prefixes and without prefixes are depicted on the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a separator in the element number

1. Open the element owner’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Separator column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the separator symbol. A new separator will override the earlier predefined separator.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates the number separator changing from “.” to “/”.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a numbering scheme

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the appropriate Numbered property row, click the Numbering Scheme cell to open the list of the defined numbering schemes.
3. Select the desired numbering scheme in that list.
4. Click OK .

When you change the numbering scheme for the selected element, the new numbering scheme is applied to all elements owned by the selected element.

The following example illustrates how numbers are depicted on the element shape after changing the numbering scheme from numerical to literal.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the last ID

1. Right-click an element numbered in the sequence, whose last ID you want to update.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The appropriate dialog opens.
3. Click Details and then click the Update Last ID button.
4. Wait while the message informs you that the last ID is set to the particular value. This value is the highest ID in the sequence.
5. Click OK to close the Element Numbering dialog.

As a result, all subsequently created elements are numbered continuing from the the highest ID. This is especially useful after you delete one or more elements from the end of the numbered sequence. In such cases, the last ID remains higher than it is expected to be, causing a numbering gap in the sequence. Updating the last ID prevents the emergence of that gap.

However, if a numbering gap is not at the end of the sequence, it remains even after the update of the last ID.

For better understanding the functionality, let's study a few cases.

1. If you have a sequence from 1 to 5, and delete elements with ID = 4 and ID = 5, the ID of a subsequently created element by default is 6. But if you update the last ID in advance, the one of the subsequently created element is 4 as it should be.
2. If you have a sequence from 1 to 3 and from 5 to 6, the last ID after the update is 6. As you can see, ID = 4 is skipped, because it isn't the highest ID in the sequence; the highest ID is 6.
