<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999158 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01082: (2026x Refresh1) Manual Task

- page_id: `303999158`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999158/2026x+Refresh1+Manual+Task
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:33.612+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Activities > (2026x Refresh1) Tasks
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486572542

1486572542

1486572542

**Description**

A Manual Task is a task that is expected to be performed without the aid of any business process execution engine or application, for example, installing a telephone at a customer location.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1486572541

**Related elements**

- Activities
- Task
- Service Task
- Send Task
- Receive Task
- User Task
- Business Rule Task
- Script Task

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using Tasks

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d2e297d-9a33-4cc0-a83b-50106cdecbca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486572542</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba516288-647c-494e-9896-afecef71c1a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486572542</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="268848ea-e1b7-44b8-9000-8e76419b5f44"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486572542</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Manual Task is a task that is expected to be performed without the aid of any business process execution engine or application, for example, installing a telephone at a customer location.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manual_task.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Manual Task" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000696 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01084: (2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues

- page_id: `304000696`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000696/2026x+Refresh1+Mapping+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:34.520+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Importing from ArchiMate
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Instance Specifications

When the elements imported from ArchiMate are mapped to the UAF Instance Specification, the predefined types from ArchiMate Customization are assigned during the import. The following table shows how the predefined types are assigned:

| # | ArchiMate 3.1 Element | UAF 1.3 |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Element | Type/Classifier | Predefined Type |  |  |
| 1. | Location | Actual Location | Location |  |
| 2. | Outcome | Actual Measurement Set | Measurement Set |  |
| 3. | Plateau | Actual Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |  |
| 4. | Stakeholder | Actual Organization | Organization |  |
| 5. | Deliverable | Actual Project Milestone | Project Milestone |  |
| 6. | Implementation Event | Actual Project Milestone | Project Milestone |  |
| 7. | Work Package | Actual Project | Project |  |

##### Relation import exceptions

There are several exceptions when importing relations from ArchiMate. The following are cases when the ArchiMate relation after the import is not directly mapped to UAF relationships, and the property is created instead of the relationship:

- Events:
  - If the ArchiMate Application Event or Technology Event is related to an ArchiMate Element using an Association, and the ArchiMate Element maps to a UAF Resource Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Resource Signal is created for the Resource Performer.
  - If ArchiMate Business Event is related to ArchiMate Element using Association, and the ArchiMate Element maps to UAF Operational Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Operational Signal is created for the Operational Performer.
- Constraints:
  - If the ArchiMate Element is related to the Constraint element using any relation, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the Constraint is applied to the UAF Element.
  - If the ArchiMate Constraint has any relationship (a) with another relationship (b) (except for the relationship that connects the Constraint with any other ArchiMate relationship), then the Constraint will be applied to the imported UAF relationship (b) during the import process, and no relationship (a) will be created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Annotated elements:
  - Any ArchiMate element related to the Grouping element using any relation, after the import is added to the UAF Element grouping [Comment] as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.
  - Any ArchiMate element related to the Notes element using Connection/Line, after the import, is added to the UAF Information [Comment] element as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.

##### Complex import and mapping depending on related ArchiMate elements and views

ArchiMate Driver and Assessment import cases:

- If ArchiMate Driver is directly related to any other PropertySet (e.g., Capability) using Association, then after the import the PropertySet with Driver as a measurement is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If the ArchiMate Assessment has no Association relation to other elements, after the import, it maps to MeasurementSet + Measurement + Default Value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If the ArchiMate Assessment name contains a value (e.g., Range=180nm) and has an Association relation with any other PropertySet (e.g., "Capability", ), but has no Association relation to Driver, then after the import Assessment is created as Measurement for PropertySet and ActualPropertySet is created with slots displaying Assessment Name and value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If ArchiMate Assessment has an Association relation with Driver and PropertySet (sequence Driver > Association > Assessment > Association > PropertySet), then after the import the Assessment is created as a Measurement for the PropertySet andActualMeasurementSets are created with slots displaying values from Assessments (slots are not created if there are no values in Assessments). Additionally, the ActualPropertySet is created for the PropertySet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Services and Interfaces:

- If ArchiMate Business Interface is related to Business Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Service with Service Port which type is Service Interface is created.
- If ArchiMate Technology/Application Interface is related to Technology/Application Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Resource Artifact with Resource Port which type is Resource Interface is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The same element is used in several ArchiMate views:

- If the same ArchiMate element is used in several ArchiMate views and can be mapped to several UAF elements according to mapping rules matching in different views (e.g., depending on relations, Archimate Plateau can be mapped either to Capability Configuration or Actual Enterprise Phase), then the element is imported and mapped to only one UAF element according to one rule.
- If certain elements, such as ArchiMate Business Processes/Functions, Application Processes/Functions, Technology Processes/Functions, Business/Application/Technology Events, or And/Or junctions, are used in multiple ArchiMate Views and are converted to activity/function + action under different domain owners (Operational/Resource/Service) and are connected using Flow or Triggering relationships, then these elements will be imported and mapped correctly according to the rules under different domain owners. However, the Flow and Triggering relationships that map to the Operational Function Control Flow or Service Function Control Flow will only be mapped according to one rule and will be owned by only one random domain owner.

##### Failed Elements and Relationships

Elements and relationships are listed as failed elements during the import from the ArchiMate model in the following cases:

- If the junctions (And, Or) are:
  - without relations
  - related to the Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event using other than Triggering relation.
  - not related to Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event elements using the Triggering relation.
- If it is any other relation than Triggering that relates to/from junctions (And/Or).
- If it is a relationship from/to failed Junction (And/Or).
- Duplicated relations.
- A relationship to itself (when source and target are the same elements):
  - The ArchiMate Specialization relationship, which after the import maps to Generalization or Containment.
  - The ArchiMate Composition relationship, which after the import maps to Containment.
- Operational Exchange between Operational Activity Actions or Resource Exchange between Function Actions if actions are created from ArchiMate Business, Application, Technology Process, or Function related to Assignment relation to Business, Application, Technology Interface.
- A relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate element. [ATTACHMENT filename='constraint_any.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The following are cases when the ArchiMate relation after the import is not directly mapped to UAF relationships, and the property is created instead of the relationship:</p><ul><li>Events:<ul><li>If the ArchiMate Application Event or Technology Event is related to an ArchiMate Element using an Association, and the ArchiMate Element maps to a UAF Resource Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Resource Signal is created for the Resource Performer.</li><li>If ArchiMate Business Event is related to ArchiMate Element using Association, and the ArchiMate Element maps to UAF Operational Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Operational Signal is created for the Operational Performer.</li></ul></li><li>Constraints:<ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If the ArchiMate Element is related to the Constraint element using any relation, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the Constraint is applied to the UAF Element.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If the ArchiMate Constraint has any relationship (a) with another relationship (b) (except for the relationship that connects the Constraint with any other ArchiMate relationship), then the Constraint will be applied to the imported UAF relationship (b) during the import process, and no relationship (a) will be created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="a_b_relations.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ul></li><li>Annotated elements:<ul><li>Any ArchiMate element related to the Grouping element using any relation, after the import is added to the UAF Element grouping [Comment] as the Annotated element. Association &gt; Assessment &gt; Association &gt; PropertySet), then after the import the Assessment is created as a Measurement for the PropertySet and </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ActualMeasurementSets are created with slots displaying values from Assessments (slots are not created if there are no values in Assessments). A<span>dditionally, the ActualPropertySet is created for the PropertySet.</span></span></span></li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assessment_driver_property_set.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Services and Interfaces:</p><ul><li>If ArchiMate Business Interface is related to Business Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Service <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">with Service Port which type is Service Interface is created.</span></li><li>If ArchiMate Technology/Application Interface is related to Technology/Application Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Resource Artifact with Resource Port which <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">type is Resource Interface is created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="technology_interface.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The same element is used in several ArchiMate views: </p><ul><li>If the same ArchiMate element is used in several ArchiMate views and can be mapped to several UAF elements according to mapping rules matching in different views (e.g., depending on relations, Archimate Plateau can be mapped either to Capability Configuration or Actual Enterprise Phase), then the element is imported and mapped to only one UAF element according to one rule.</li><li>If certain elements, such as ArchiMate Business Processes/Functions, activity/function + action under different domain owners (Operational/Resource/Service) and are connected using Flow or Triggering relationships, then these elements will be imported and mapped correctly according to the rules under different domain owners. However, the Flow and Triggering relationships that map to the Operational Function Control Flow or Service Function Control Flow will only be mapped according to one rule and will be owned by only one random domain owner.</li></ul><h3>Failed Elements and Relationships</h3><p>Elements and relationships are listed as failed elements during the import from the ArchiMate model in the following cases:</p><ul><li>If the junctions (And, Or) are:<ul><li>without relations </li><li>related to the Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event using <strong>other</strong> than Triggering relation.</li><li>not related to Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event elements using the Triggering relation.</li></ul></li><li>If it is any other relation than Triggering that relates to/from junctions (And/Or).</li><li>If it is a relationship from/to failed Junction (And/Or).</li><li>Duplicated relations.</li><li>A relationship to itself (when source and target are the same elements):<ul><li>The ArchiMate Specialization relationship, which after the import maps to Generalization or Containment.</li><li>The ArchiMate Composition relationship, which after the import maps to Containment.</li></ul></li><li>Operational Exchange between Operational Activity Actions or Resource Exchange between Function Actions if actions are created from ArchiMate Business, Application, Technology Process, or Function related to Assignment relation to Business, Application, Technology Interface.</li><li>A relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint_any.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992763 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01086: (2026x Refresh1) Mapping ports on a Part type change

- page_id: `303992763`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992763/2026x+Refresh1+Mapping+ports+on+a+Part+type+change
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:18.357+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

On a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED'] type change, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED'] of the original Part type are automatically replaced with ports of a new type. Connectors are reconnected accordingly too.

Ports are mapped automatically if particular rules are met. Otherwise, the **Port Mapping** dialog appears.

##### Rules of automatic port mapping

Ports are mapped automatically:

- When the following properties of the Port of the original Part type are the same as the properties of the Port of the new part type:
  - Port type and name
  - Port type (note that only one Port of the new Part type has the same type as Port(s) of the original Part type)
  - Port name (valid if the type of the original Port is not specified)
- Ports of the original Part type and the Port of the new Part type has the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MED'] .
- When the new Part type is subtype of the original Part type (inherited ports are mapped automatically with ports which they are inherited).
- When the ports of a new type redefines ports of original Part type.

##### The Port Mapping dialog

The **Port Mapping** dialog appears on changing a Part type when ports of the type cannot be mapped automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the elements of the **Port Mapping** dialog described in the following table.

| Element number | Element | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 |  | Lists the type of the Part from which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 2 |  | Lists the type of the Part to which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 3 |  | Displays lines between the mapped ports. |
| 4 |  | Creates a new port in the To list. In the From list, click a port and then drag it to the "Drag port(s) here to create a new port" box. The port for the new type will be created and mapped. |

To map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto a Port in the **To** box. The ports are mapped and the line between them is displayed. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto a port in the **To** box. The selected ports will be mapped with the Port.

To create a new Port in the **To** box

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto the following box: "*Drag**port(s)**here**to**create**a**new**port*". The new Port is created in the **To** box and ports are mapped. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto the box: "*Drag port(s) here to create a new port*". The selected number of ports will be created.

To un-map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select the line between the mapped ports.
2. Press the Delete key. The line is deleted and ports unmapped.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>On a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part" /></ac:link> type change, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the original Part type are automatically replaced with ports of a new type. Connectors are reconnected accordingly too.</p><p>Ports are mapped automatically if particular rules are met. Otherwise, the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog appears.</p><h3>Rules of automatic port mapping</h3><p>Ports are mapped automatically:</p><ul><li>When the following properties of the Port of the original Part type are the same as the properties of the Port of the new part type:<ul><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port type and name</strong></li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port </strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">type</strong> (note that only one Port of the new Part type has the same type as Port(s) of the original Part type) </li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Port name</strong> (valid if the type of the original Port is <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">not</strong> specified) </li></ul></li><li>Ports of the original Part type and the Port of the new Part type has the same <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interface" /></ac:link>. </li><li>When the new Part type is subtype of the original Part type (inherited ports are mapped automatically with ports which they are inherited).</li><li>When the ports of a new type redefines ports of original Part type. </li></ul><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>The Port Mapping dialog</h3><p>The <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog appears on changing a Part type when ports of the type cannot be mapped automatically. </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Port Mapping dialog" ac:alt="Port Mapping dialog"><ri:attachment elements of the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog described in the following table.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Element number</th><th>Element</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>1</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Lists the type of the Part from which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type.</td></tr><tr><td>2</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Lists the type of the Part to which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type.</td></tr><tr><td>3</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Displays lines between the mapped ports.</td></tr><tr><td>4</td><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_mapping2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Creates a new port in the <strong>To</strong> list. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />In the <strong>From</strong> list, click a port and then drag it to the &quot;Drag port(s) here to create a new port&quot; box. The port for the new type will be created and mapped.</td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>To map ports in the <strong>Port Mapping</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port in the <strong>From</strong> box.</li><li><p>Drag the selected Port onto a Port in the <strong>To</strong> box. The ports are mapped and the line between them is displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2018e296-0847-4d0a-8481-8d55c293ae7f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select multiple number of ports in the <strong>From</strong> box and drag onto a port in the <strong>To</strong> box. The new Port is created in the <strong>To</strong> box and ports are mapped.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc09ca29-20ea-4daa-b80c-d08fa53fe280"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also select multiple number of ports in the <strong>From</strong> box and drag onto the box: &quot;<em>Drag port(s) here to create a new port</em>&quot;. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996846 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01087: (2026x Refresh1) Mapping Problems and Solutions

- page_id: `303996846`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996846/2026x+Refresh1+Mapping+Problems+and+Solutions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:29.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using To Do Property

Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked “added to the toDo property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration will be placed in the element's To Do property.

For instance, if you had Supports Operational Activity before the migration, the information about it will be stored in the ToDo property of Service Interface (source of the relationship).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Additionally to the fact that the Service Interface supported Operational Activity, Supports Operational Activity relationship name and other UPDM properties that had values assigned will be added to the toDo property.

Use the **Find ToDo** command to search for elements containing **ToDo** information. From the **Edit** main menu, choose **Find ToDo**.

##### Displaying Related Elements

Newly created relationships are not displayed in the diagrams by default. The reason for this is to prevent the damage on the diagrams. However, you can easily visualize the information, existing in the model, on the diagram of your choice.

To easily display newly created relationships, select the element that is the source or the target of the relationship and from the shortcut menu select **Related Elements** > **Display Related Elements**.

For instance, relationship between Entity Item and Information Element has been realized through a representedBy property of the Entity Item. It is now changed to the relationship Details. However in diagram relationships are not displayed by default. In order to display the relationships, right-click one Entity Item at a time and from the shortcut menu select **Related Elements** > **Display Related Elements**.

Make sure the Dependency «Details» entry is checked in the**Display Related Elements** dialog and click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Related elements and relationships appear on the diagram.

##### Preserving Needline and Resource Interface Data

According to UPDM 2.0, specification Needlines and Resource Interfaces can only be used in a particular context such as Logical or Physical Architecture or simply inside the another Performer/Node. In other words, these relationships can only be used to model internal structures.

To preserve the data of the Needlines and Resource Interfaces, Needlines are changed to Node Associations and Resource Interfaces to Resource Associations. From the first sight, differences on the diagram are barely visible.

Node Association and Resource Association both are not a part of UPDM standard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Using To Do Property</h3><p>Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked “added to the toDo property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration will be placed in the element's To Do property.</p><p>For instance, if you had Supports Operational Activity before the migration, the information about it will be stored in the ToDo property of Service Interface (source of the relationship).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service interface.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping Problems and Solutions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Additionally to the fact that the Service Interface supported Operational Activity, Supports Operational Activity relationship name and other UPDM properties that had values assigned will be added to the toDo property.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="156a7e02-3678-403a-97ac-94ad52382c26"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the <strong>Find ToDo</strong> command to search for elements containing <strong>ToDo</strong> information. From the <strong>Edit</strong> main menu, choose <strong>Find ToDo</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Displaying Related Elements</h3><p>Newly created relationships are not displayed in the diagrams by default. The reason for this is to prevent the damage on the diagrams. However, you can easily visualize the information, existing in the model, on the diagram of your choice.</p><p>To easily display newly created relationships, select the element that is the source or the target of the relationship and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><p>For instance, relationship between Entity Item and Information Element has been realized through a representedBy property of the Entity Item. It is now changed to the relationship Details. However in diagram relationships are not displayed by default. In order to display the relationships, right-click one Entity Item at a time and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><p>Make sure the Dependency «Details» entry is checked in the<strong> Display Related Elements</strong> dialog and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="related elements.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping Problems and Solutions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Related elements and relationships appear on the diagram. </p><h3>Preserving Needline and Resource Interface Data</h3><p>According to UPDM 2.0, specification Needlines and Resource Interfaces can only be used in a particular context such as Logical or Physical Architecture or simply inside the another Performer/Node. In other words, these relationships can only be used to model internal structures.</p><p>To preserve the data of the Needlines and Resource Interfaces, Needlines are changed to Node Associations and Resource Interfaces to Resource Associations. From the first sight, differences on the diagram are barely visible.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="205855c3-1064-46a1-9b9f-e4f4deffe96d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Node Association and Resource Association both are not a part of UPDM standard.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996786 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01088: (2026x Refresh1) Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `303996786`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996786/2026x+Refresh1+Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:22.294+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

##### Changed Property Types

44

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for :

- Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.
- Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created . I f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

##### The SecurityProperty renamed

In UAF 1.1, the Security Property is renamed to Information Role and the new Data Role is implemented. The Information Role is used by the Operational Assets and the Data Role is used by the Resource Assets.

- Operational Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Information Element.
- Resource Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Data Element.

If Operational Agent and Information Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Operational Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was any of the Operational Asset specializations.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If Resource Performer and Data Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Resource Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Data Role, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Operational Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Known Resource exception

Known Resource exception

The Known Resource is both: Resource Asset and Operational Asset. After the migration, the Security Property (owned by the Know Resource) is changed depending on its type:

- If the type was Data Element, the Security Property becomes Data Role.
- If the type was Information Element, the Security Property becomes Information Role.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this propert<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7a243fc5-0832-49c4-9bb5-f64004a2f9e1">y.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>The same is valid for :</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e3a0d88f-532d-4e71-9035-36fc184b61f8">Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b75db304-ea1e-44e3-89a2-42ec9b4bfa48">Required</ac:inline-comment-marker> Environment (owned by Location Holder) value.  If the value was <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6373d10f-6adf-4853-886e-c879c59757c4">Environment, the Actual Environment is created</ac:inline-comment-marker>. I<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.</span></li></ul><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="concition_to_instance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Changes for Doctrine</h4><p>In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995374 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01089: (2026x Refresh1) Marking elements as auxiliary

- page_id: `303995374`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995374/2026x+Refresh1+Marking+elements+as+auxiliary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:29.116+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639285959

639285961

639285960

An auxiliary element is an element needed in the model but not necessarily visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space='']. Usually some profiles are marked as auxiliary and are not displayed by default in the model. You can mark the element in your model as an auxiliary as well.

To mark an element as auxiliary

1. Select the element in the Model Browser.
2. In the Properties tab, select the Tags tab.
3. Set the treatAsAuxiliaryInOwningProject tag value to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting the tag value.

The element marked as auxiliary is visible in the Model Browser if the option **Show Auxiliary Resources**is selected in the Options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Options drop-down list.

You can specify that all auxiliary resources are shown in the model by default by specifying in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space='']**of your tool.

To show auxiliary resources by default in your model

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment .
2. In the search box, type auxiliary . All auxiliary resources related properties appears in the options list.
3. Set the Show Auxiliary Resources property value to true .

639285958

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989922 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01090: (2026x Refresh1) MaxExclusive

- page_id: `303989922`
- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 1

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDmaxExclusive. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: maxExclusive XML representation summary

maxExclusive XML representation summary

```text
<maxExclusive
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = anySimpleType
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>	Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</maxExclusive>
```

```text
{value} must be in the value space of {base type definition}.
```

#### PANEL: maxExclusive XML code sample

maxExclusive XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which limits values to integers less than or equal to 100, using maxExclusive.
```

```text
<simpleType name='less-than-one-hundred-and-one'›
```

```text
<restriction base='integer'›
```

```text
<maxExclusive value='101'/>
```

```text
</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType›
```

```text
Note that the value space of this datatype is identical to the previous one (named 'one­-hundred-or-less').
```

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDmaxLength. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: maxLength XML representation summary

maxLength XML representation summary

```text
<maxLength
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = nonNegativeInteger
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</maxLength>
```

#### PANEL: maxLength XML code sample

maxLength XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which might be used to accept form input with an upper limit to the number of characters that are acceptable.<simpleType name='form-input'>	<restriction base='string'>		<maxLength value='50'/>	</restriction></simpleType>
```

## PAGE 01093: (2026x Refresh1) Mean Concept Relationships

- page_id: `303991909`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991909/2026x+Refresh1+Mean+Concept+Relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:04.550+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) Business Motivation Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Means Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487501502

1487501504

1487501504

The following table provides the relationships that exist between element types and their meaning.

| Relationship | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Makes Operative | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Mission lists every continuingActivity to ensure the Vision. |  |
| Component Of | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Strategy is the method orcourse of action that will be employed to achieve the Mission. |  |
| Implements | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Tactic realizes Strategies. |  |
| Enables | This link connects to a or to a . Meaning that a Strategy/Tactic makes another Strategy/Tactic doable. That is to say the latter Strategy/Tacticprovides an opportunity for the former Strategy/Tactic to be carried out. Use this linkto associate Strategy to another Strategy or Tactic to another Tactic. |  |
| Effects Enforcement Level | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Tactics influences the |  |
| Formulated Based On | This link connects or to a or . Meaningthat the Strategy or Tactic is planned according to what result the Business Policy orBusiness Rule desires. |  |
| Channel Efforts Towards | This link connects to a , or to an . Meaning that the Strategy iscoordinated as activities aimed towards Goals as Tactic towards Objective. For example, theTactic “Free delivery” channels efforts towards the Objective “5% increase in sales within 3months.” |  |
| Governs | This link connects or to a or . Meaning that the Business Policy or BusinessRule determines the Strategy or Tactic. |  |
| Basis For | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Business Policy is the key to achieve Business Rule. |  |
| Supports Achievement Of | This link connects or to a or . Meaning that the Business Policy provides anidea or reason to develop the Strategy or Tactic. |  |
| Acts As Regulation | This link connects a or a to External Influencer. |  |

1487501501

**Related element**

- Means Concepts

**Related diagrams**

- Business Motivation Diagram
- Process Definition Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993566 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01095: (2026x Refresh1) Measurements

- page_id: `303993566`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993566/2026x+Refresh1+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:17.702+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .

Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the **Show Original Name of Measurement** symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Learn more about working with measurements:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .</p><p>Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the <strong>Show Original Name of Measurement</strong> symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_measurements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="original_measurement_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Learn more about working with measurements:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c2b0623-cd0e-4255-b0a4-ebeb8a94756b" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999983/2026x+Refresh1+Measurements+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:32.880+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['measurements-dialog', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004957137

1004957139

1004957138

The **Measurements** dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] tables.

To open the **Measurements** dialog

- On the table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Measurements . The Measurements dialog opens.

The **Measurements** dialog consists of two tabs:

- Operational Exchange (or **Resource****Exchange** in the SV-6 table).
- Operational Exchange Item (or Resource ExchangeItem in the SV-6 table).

The**Operational Exchange** (or **Resource****Exchange**in the SV-6 and table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Exchanges in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The **Operational Exchange Item** (or**Resource ExchangeItem** in the SV-6 table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Exchange Items in the SV-6 table). The measurements displayed in the **Measurements** dialog are collected from the active table.

The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.

When you select the measurement and click the **OK** button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.

The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004957135

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying Custom Measurements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999201 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01098: (2026x Refresh1) Measurements migration

- page_id: `303999201`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999201/2026x+Refresh1+Measurements+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:40.551+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > (2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.
- Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.

If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.</p><p>The new approach includes:</p><ul><li>If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.</li><li>If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.</li><li>Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_migration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Measurements migration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.</h6><p>If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.</p>
````

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999577 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 01100: (2026x Refresh1) Memory allocation

- page_id: `303999577`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999577/2026x+Refresh1+Memory+allocation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-25T13:47:27.158+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Low memory notification

1427463063

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Low memory notification

1427463065

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Low memory notification

1427463064

If you receive an error message saying your modeling tool is out of memory, the application does not have enough memory to operate. You can change the amount of allocated memory directly in the error message dialog or by changing modeling tool properties.

#### TIP: Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation for the product is the same as the Java heap size. To increase the amount of memory allocated for the product, simply increase the Java heap size.

##### Recommended amount of allocated memory

The amount of allocated memory (or Java heap size) should be set to less than the available physical RAM on your computer. To calculate the approximate amount of memory you should allocate, subtract the amount of memory taken by any other processes that will run concurrently with your modeling tool from the total amount of RAM in your computer. Otherwise, your JVM process will likely swap, and that will slow down the application.

##### Changing the amount of allocated memory

You can set the amount of memory allocated for your modeling tool either in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog, in the modeling tool properties file, or in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Options** dialog

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the Memory Settings category.
3. In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the Maximum Heap Size option. You should only change the value of the Thread Stack Size option if a *java.lang.StackOverflowError*error occurs.

#### TIP: Low memory notification

Low memory notification

While working on the project, you can get the *Low memory* notification. This notification is triggered by the mechanism which tracks whether the allocated amount of memory is enough to work with your currently opened projects. Click *Open Environment Options*to open the **Environment options** dialog where you can adjust memory settings.

To enable/disable notifications about low memory

1. Go to Environment Options > Display > Notify about low memory option

- 
  - Set to true to show notifications about low memory.
  - Set to false to disable notifications about low memory. Also, t his notification can be disabled, by clicking Do not show again option in the Low memory notification.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the modeling tool properties file

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the modeling tool properties file.
2. In the **JAVA_ARGS** line, change the value of the **-Xmx** property. For example, change the **-Xmx800M** to **-Xmx1066M**.
3. Save and close the file.
4. Restart your modeling tool.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine

1. Go to Control Panel > System and Security > System > Advanced system settings. The System Properties dialog opens.
2. In the Advanced tab, click Environment Variables . The Environment Variables dialog opens.
3. In the System variables section, do one of the following:
  1. If the _JAVA_OPTIONS variable is not in the variables list, click New . New System Variable dialog opens.
  2. If the **_JAVA_OPTIONS** variable is in the variables list, select it and click **Edit**.**Edit System Variable**dialog opens.
4. Set **Variable name** to **_JAVA_OPTIONS** and **Variable value** to the desired memory amount, e.g. **-Xmx1066M**. Click **OK**. Changing options via the **Environment Variables** dialog overrides specified options for all Java applications.

1427463062

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Background Task Manager' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Active validation period' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba5d00a9-bef0-475e-999a-12d56751127a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463063</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ca76881-7612-41c9-b6e9-e63076a33419"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463065</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b761f68-7235-4784-9d55-8dad2ef45222"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463064</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you receive an error message saying your modeling tool is out of memory, the application does not have enough memory to operate. You can change the amount of allocated memory directly in the error message dialog or by changing modeling tool properties.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b22dbf74-6683-4398-83b1-fc0c6753efad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory allocation concept</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Memory allocation for the product is the same as the Java heap size. To increase the amount of memory allocated for the product, simply increase the Java heap size.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Recommended amount of allocated memory</h3><p>The amount of allocated memory (or Java heap size) should be set to less than the available physical RAM on your computer. To calculate the approximate amount of memory you should allocate, subtract the amount of memory taken by any other processes that will run concurrently with your modeling tool from the total amount of RAM in your computer. Otherwise, your JVM process will likely swap, and that will slow down the application.</p><h3>Changing the amount of allocated memory</h3><p>You can set the amount of memory allocated for your modeling tool either in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, in the modeling tool properties file, or in the <strong>Environment Variables</strong> dialog on your machine.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the amount of allocated memory in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Expand the <strong>General</strong> options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the <strong>Memory Settings</strong> category.</li><li data-uuid="8b11f905-7013-4d3d-acad-e7eb79c5f146">In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the <strong>Maximum Heap Size </strong>option. You should only change the value of the <strong>Thread Stack Size </strong><span>option if a <em>java.lang.StackOverflowError </em>error occurs.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ba52b51-1396-48a9-872b-1e7e97404c59"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Low memory notification</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>While working on the project, you can get the <em>Low memory</em> notification. This notification is triggered by the mechanism which tracks whether the allocated amount of memory is enough to work with your currently opened projects. Click <em>Open Environment Options </em>to open the <strong>Environment options</strong> dialog where you can adjust memory settings.</p><p>To enable/disable notifications about low memory</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to<strong> Environment Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Display </strong>&gt;<strong> Notify about low memory option</strong></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Set to <em>true</em> to show notifications about low memory.</li><li><span>Set to </span><em>false</em><span> to disable notifications about low memory. Also, t</span>his notification can be disabled, by clicking <em>Do not show again</em><span> option </span>in the <strong>Low memory</strong> notification.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the amount of allocated memory in the modeling tool properties file</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the modeling tool properties file.</li><li><p>In the <strong>JAVA_ARGS</strong> line, change the value of the <strong>-Xmx</strong> property. For example, change the <strong>-Xmx800M</strong> to <strong>-Xmx1066M</strong>.</p></li><li>Save and close the file.</li><li>Restart your modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To change the amount of allocated memory in the <strong>Environment Variables</strong> dialog on your machine</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to <strong>Control Panel</strong> &gt; <strong>System and Security </strong>&gt; <strong>System</strong> &gt; <strong>Advanced system settings. </strong>The<strong> System Properties </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Advanced</strong> tab, click <strong>Environment Variables</strong>. The <strong>Environment Variables </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>System variables</strong> section, do one of the following:<ol><li>If the <strong>_JAVA_OPTIONS</strong> variable is not in the variables list, click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">New</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">New System Variable</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog opens.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If the <strong>_JAVA_OPTIONS</strong> variable is in the variables list, select it and click <strong>Edit</strong><span>. </span><strong>Edit System Variable</strong><span> dialog opens.</span><br /></span></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set <strong>Variable name</strong> to <strong>_JAVA_OPTIONS</strong> and <strong>Variable value</strong> to the desired memory amount, e.g. <strong>-Xmx1066M</strong>. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ebb2a11-1dd9-4e35-ac23-bbd208087f43"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Changing options via the <strong>Environment Variables</strong> dialog overrides specified options for all Java applications.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2414574d-e565-492d-bb3e-b8c604af9e9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427463062</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Improving performance" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Background Task Manager" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Active validation period" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000117 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01101: (2026x Refresh1) Memory and Disk Space Requirements for Large Models

- page_id: `304000117`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000117/2026x+Refresh1+Memory+and+Disk+Space+Requirements+for+Large+Models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:48.641+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Guidelines for working with large models
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Memory

The memory allocation for the modeling tool by project size:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Allocated Memory 2 |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Earlier versions | 2021x and later 1 |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 7GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 11GB |

The amount of required memory proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

1the project elements count is different due to the profiling data storing metamodel optimized in 2021x (decrease by 40-80% in SysML v1 and UAF projects).

2these memory requirements are indicative and may vary by project specifics.

The operations that require more memory (1, 2, and 3.5 million of 2021x project elements respectively):

- UPDM migration to UAF - 10GB, 10GB, 36GB
- Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB
- Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB 3

3strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).

##### Disk Space

The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Disk Space |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 19.0 | 2021x |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 10GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 30GB |

The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

**Note:** Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.

Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

10GB, 10GB, 36GB</li><li>Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB</li><li>Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB <sup>3</sup></li></ul><p><sup>3 </sup>strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).</p><h3>Disk Space</h3><p>The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files: </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">Project Elements Count<br />(including used projects)</th><th style="text-align: center;" rowspan="2">Disk Space</th></tr><tr><th style="text-align: center;">19.0</th><th style="text-align: center;">2021x</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">1 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">6GB</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;">2.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">2 million</td><td style="text-align: center;">10GB</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">3.5 million</td><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">30GB</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.</p><p><strong>Note:</strong> Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.</p><p>Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999675 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01102: (2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor

- page_id: `303999675`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999675/2026x+Refresh1+Memory+monitor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:12.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Running out of memory

1427409680

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Running out of memory

1427409682

238600894

**On this page:**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Running out of memory

1427409681

When working with larger models, turn on the Memory Monitor to monitor the memory used by a modeling tool. The Memory Monitor shows currently used memory and the current Java heap size. Typically, when you start working with a model, used memory increases because actions are stored in the undo list, and opened diagrams are loaded.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The memory monitor bar showing currently used memory and the current Java heap size.

##### Turning on the Memory Monitor

To turn on the Memory Monitor

- In the main menu of a modeling tool, select View > Status Line > Show Memory Monitor . The Memory Monitor bar appears in the bottom-right corner of the modeling tool window.

#### WARNING: Running out of memory

Running out of memory

When used memory takes more than 85% of the total heap size, the Memory Monitor bar turns red, indicating that a modeling tool may run out of memory soon.

##### Freeing unused memory with the Garbage Collector

If a modeling tool uses all the Java heap size, you can try to recover enough memory for saving a project with the Garbage Collector, but this may take some time.

To free unused memory with the Garbage Collector

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar and select Run Garbage Collector .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Memory Monitor pop-up window

If you find it impossible to use the modeling tool and/or see the Out of Memory error, you should dump the memory heap to the**.hprof**file that is created in the defined location on the computer and then submit this file to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC'] for further assistance regarding the memory consumption issue.

##### Dumping the memory heap

To dump the memory heap

Do one of the following:

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select Dump Memory Heap , and then set the desired name and location of the memory heap file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp.
- Press Ctrl+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory heap file.

You must create a new file for the heap dump since the memory heap cannot be dumped into an existing file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dumping memory heap

If the Memory Monitor bar is still red, save the model, restart the modeling tool, and reload the model. If the Memory Monitor bar stays red after reloading the model, you should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Memory allocation' space=''].

##### Dumping the memory histogram

To dump the memory histogram

Do one of the following:

- Click anywhere on the Memory Monitor bar, select Dump Memory Histogram , and then set the desired name and location of the memory histogram file. By default, the name of the file is generated according to the current timestamp.
- Press Ctrl+Alt+Shift+H on your keyboard and then define the name and location of the generated memory histogram file. To use the **Dump Memory Histogram** functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dumping memory histogram

1427409679

## PAGE 01103: (2026x Refresh1) Message Boundary Event

- page_id: `303991793`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991793/2026x+Refresh1+Message+Boundary+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:15.897+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Boundary Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486349062

1486349065

1486349065

**Description**

A Message Boundary Event is triggered by an arrived message. Once triggered, it changes a normal flow into an exception flow or parallel.

A Message Boundary Event displays any of the following on the diagram:

- A Message Boundary Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation**

- Message Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - True) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_message.png']
- Message Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - False) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_message_cancelactivity.png']

1486349061

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Boundary Events' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activities' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) BPMNMessage' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998884 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01104: (2026x Refresh1) Message Catching Intermediate Event

- page_id: `303998884`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998884/2026x+Refresh1+Message+Catching+Intermediate+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:30.535+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Intermediate Catch Event
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Message Catching Intermediate Event is used to receive a message.

This event causes a process to continue if it is waiting for the message.

A Message Catching Intermediate Event displays any of the following on a diagram:

- A Message Catching Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']
- Intermediate Catch Event

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01105: (2026x Refresh1) Message End Event

- page_id: `303998905`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998905/2026x+Refresh1+Message+End+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:28.489+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) End Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Message End Event indicates that a message will be sent when a process is completed.

This event displays any of the following on a diagram:

- A Message End Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- End Events
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990522 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01106: (2026x Refresh1) Message Flow

- page_id: `303990522`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990522/2026x+Refresh1+Message+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:27.458+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486264927

1486264928

1486264927

Description

A Message Flow is used to show the flow of messages between two participants who are prepared to send and receive them.

- A Message Flow must connect separate Pools. It can be connected to the pool boundary or an element inside the pool.
- A Message Flow cannot connect two elements in the same Pool.

Messages that are sent by a Message Flow can be displayed on a diagram in two ways:

- overlapping the Message Flow
- associated with the Message Flow

However, they can also be hidden.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Message Flows between two pools.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Messages overlapping Message Flows

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Message Flows between Pools Inner elements

1486264925

**Related element**

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998516 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01108: (2026x Refresh1) Message Start Event

- page_id: `303998516`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998516/2026x+Refresh1+Message+Start+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:36.990+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Start Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Message Start Event means that a message from a participant has arrived and triggered the start of a process.

A Message Start Event displays any of the following on a diagram:

- A Message Start Event name if the name is specified.
- A Message Ref property value if the name is unspecified.
- An Operation Ref property if the name and Message Ref are not specified.

**Notation**

- Interrupting Message Start Event [ATTACHMENT filename='start_event_interrupting.png']
- Non-interrupting Message Start Event [ATTACHMENT filename='start_event_nonint.png']

**Related elements**

- Start Events
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']
