<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990419 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01112: (2026x Refresh1) Method specification report

- page_id: `303990419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990419/2026x+Refresh1+Method+specification+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:28.177+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Use Case driven
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the Method Specification report is to show the method design and its related use cases in detail to developers.

The report shows a class diagram and a list of all classes in the diagram. The list contains attributes and methods for each class. The report also shows the method specification, parameters, and algorithms.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The purpose of the Method Specification report is to show the method design and its related use cases in detail to developers.</p><p>The report shows a class diagram and a list of all classes in the diagram. The list contains attributes and methods for each class. The report also shows the method specification, parameters, and algorithms.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995451 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01113: (2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites

- page_id: `303995451`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995451/2026x+Refresh1+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:35.427+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1497238846

1497238847

1895909674

**On this page**

4

1497238847

A metric is the item of information about the specific aspect of a model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of the model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help to track the evolution of model development.

800450

For better understanding of further material, get acquainted with the following concepts

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Metric Suite | A collection of parameter and metric definitions used to calculate the metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model. |
| Parameter definition | The variable that is used to calculate metrics. It can be an element type (including both relationship and DSL element types), particular element, data type, or data type value. |
| Metric definition | The attribute of a Metric Suite defining the formula for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |
| Validation-based metric definition | The attribute of a Metric Suite specifying the validation rule for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |

In general, a Metric Suite is a Class with the «MetricSuite» stereotype applied. Both parameter and metric definitions are attributes of a Metric Suite.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

One metric is the result of a single calculation of a metric definition. The metrics of all the metric definitions of a Metric Suite together with relevant parameters are stored in a single instance of a Metric Suite (as slot values). To analyse and manage instances of a Metric Suite with a modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric table' space=''].

##### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
Creating Metric Suites

There are several conventions that you must know about Metric Suites before creating one:

- Metric Suite must have a target. A target specifies model elements, for which metrics of the particular metric suite can be calculated. The target value can be any type of elements or even a particular element of your model.
- Metric Suite must have one parameter definition declared as context. The element, which is selected for calculating metrics, is automatically set as the value of the context parameter definition.
- Metric Suite must have a date tracker, so that you could see the timestamp of each metric.

To create and define a Metric Suite, complete the following steps:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding a new Metric Suite' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space=''] .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying parameter definitions' space=''] (including the context parameter definition).
4. Build [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Building metric definitions' space=''] and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Building validation-based metric definitions' space=''] .

It’s recommended to have a single person per project, who is responsible for creating and managing all the Metric Suites of the project.

1497238846

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specific use cases' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994302 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01114: (2026x Refresh1) Metric table

- page_id: `303994302`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994302/2026x+Refresh1+Metric+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:08.497+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

222736122

222736125

222736121

**On this page**

222736124

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Architect and Enterprise editions only.

The Metric Table is one of four tables. The Metric Table allows you to collect various metrics used to evaluate the current state of your model. A Metric is an item of information about a specific aspect of the model. Each row in the table represents a set of metrics calculated according to the metric suites specified by the user.

To understand the material better, you need to be familiar with the following concepts.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Metric suite | A collection of parameter and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model. |
| Parameter definition | A variable used for calculating metrics. It can be an element type (including both relationship and DSL element types), a particular element, data type, or data type value. |
| Metric definition | A formula for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |
| Validation-based metric definition | The attribute of a Metric Suite specifying the validation rule for calculating metrics according to the parameter values. |

Actually, a metric suite is a class with the *«MetricSuite»* stereotype applied. Both parameter and metric definitions are attributes of the metric suite.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Purpose**

Calculated at regular intervals, metrics help track the evolution of the model development. One metric is the result of a single calculation of a metric definition. The metrics of all the metric definitions of the metric suite, together with relevant parameters, are stored in a single instance of the metric suite as slot values. Use the Metric Table to analyze and manage the instances of the metric suite.

With a help of a Metric Table you can:

- Calculate model metrics.
- Change the parameters of the metrics and recalculate them.
- Customize the representation of the metrics.
- Export the data to an .html , .xlsx , or .csv file.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Specific tasks in the Metric Table**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991108 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01115: (2026x Refresh1) Metrics

- page_id: `303991108`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991108/2026x+Refresh1+Metrics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:21.402+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing Requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A metric is information about a specific aspect of the model. A collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. When calculated at regular intervals, they can help track the evolution of the model's development.

There are two metric suites dedicated to coverage analysis:

- The Design metric suite checks incoming satisfy relations.
- The Test cases metric suite checks incoming verify relations.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of metrics table.

When working with requirements, it is useful to know what and how many requirements are covered by design elements (satisfy relations), by test cases (verify relations), are user requirements covered with system requirements, and how many requirements are already implemented or tested. In these situations metrics help you evaluate the current state of requirements in your model and track the progress of their coverage.

The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element) is considered as covered if it satisfies one of the following conditions:

- A requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element.
- The owning requirement is covered.
- All owned requirements are covered.

The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement) is considered as covered if the requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element:

- If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relations, grouping requirements are not considered as satisfied unless they also have incoming satisfy relations.
- If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not considered as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Examples of covered requirements.

To use coverage analysis metrics

1. Select the package that includes the requirements.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Metrics > New Metric Table .
3. In the Create Metric Table dialog, set to true one of the metric suite:
  - Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element).
  - Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement).
4. Click OK . The new metric table with calculated results is created.

You can create custom metric suites using structured expressions or scripts. For detailed procedures on creating metric suites, calculating metrics, and managing metric results, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites' space='']Creating Metric Suites and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric table' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A metric is information about a specific aspect of the model. A collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. When calculated at regular intervals, they can help track the evolution of the model's development.</p><p>There are two metric suites dedicated to coverage analysis:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4dce7850-45d7-4aa9-a3b7-a4643916e0f3">The Design metric suite checks incoming satisfy relations.</li><li data-uuid="f3cc3224-3b7d-42c4-a1db-383c756130e1">The Test cases metric suite checks incoming verify relations.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metrics_table_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Metrics" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Example of metrics table.</h6><p>When working with requirements, it is useful to know what and how many requirements are covered by design elements (satisfy relations), by test cases (verify relations), are user requirements covered with system requirements, and how many requirements are already implemented or tested. In these situations metrics help you evaluate the current state of requirements in your model and track the progress of their coverage.</p><p>The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element) is considered as covered if it satisfies one of the following conditions:</p><ul><li data-uuid="14a3fbaa-23bc-4bcb-b1ce-90cf5890a5ae">A requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element.</li><li data-uuid="85e7fe5e-f7b3-4590-a4fb-f5061ff96496">The owning requirement is covered.</li><li data-uuid="154ef613-11b5-47bc-b4cd-a912747a5a8d">All owned requirements are covered.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />The metric suite Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement) is considered as covered if the requirement has a satisfy relation with a design element:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e3d5bb46-3f75-4f59-b4c0-a8d27869e4d5">If all child requirements have incoming satisfy relations, grouping requirements are not considered as satisfied unless they also have incoming satisfy relations.</li><li data-uuid="05bb3d53-dae7-42a3-895e-d14493299637">If the grouping requirement has incoming satisfy relation, its child requirements are not considered as covered unless they have incoming satisfy relations.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="examples_of_covered_requirements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Metrics" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Examples of covered requirements.</h6><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To use coverage analysis metrics</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="70bc75db-8f5c-47cc-8542-757a8a5eb3d1">Select the package that includes the requirements.</li><li data-uuid="a7d94ab0-fcbe-403e-90f8-76af3678af11">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Metrics</strong> &gt; <strong>New </strong><strong>Metric</strong><strong> Table</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="4cefb6e4-ba80-40e9-85d5-3fa0d7dc17ac">In the <strong>Create</strong><strong> Metric</strong><strong> Table </strong>dialog, set to <em>true</em> one of the metric suite:<br /><ul><li>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Grouping Element).</li><li>Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner as Regular Requirement).</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b5b9e253-31a8-4897-b7bc-dfb4ef7f6101">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The new metric table with calculated results is created.</li></ol><p>You can create custom metric suites using structured expressions or scripts. For detailed procedures on creating metric suites, calculating metrics, and managing metric results, please see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Creating Metric</span> Suites</ac:link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Metric table" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"><span>.</span></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999707 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01116: (2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)

- page_id: `303999707`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999707/2026x+Refresh1+Microsoft+Office+Excel+worksheet+XLSX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:13.293+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix B: office open XML format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Multi-Line Statements in *XLSX*

All multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions.

The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single cell. The figures below show samples of invalid usage of the **#if** and **#foreach** statements between cells respectively.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Executing multi-line statements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid usage of #foreach statements.

In the first figure, since the body of the #if statement ($e.name) resides in the A2 cell, not in the A1 cell, the body will not be generated when generating a report, regardless of the evaluation of the #if statement. The code shown in the second figure will break the structure of a spreadsheet document.

The following two figures demonstrate samples of valid usage of the #if and #foreach statements respectively.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Valid usage of an #if statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Valid usage of a #foreach statement.

- A VTL Macro must be declared within a single cell. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single cell, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid usage of a VTL Macro.

The macro will copy all contents between #macro and #end. Cells and rows will be included in the macro as well. Once this record has been inserted, the macro content will break the document structure.

The following figure demonstrates a sample of valid usage of the #macro statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Valid usage of a VTL Macro.

#### Creating data for multiple rows

The **#foreach** directive can only be used in a single cell record. To create data for multiple rows, use the **#forrow** directive instead (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The forrow directive.

The figure below demonstrates the output of the above code.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from figure 7.

#### Creating data for multiple columns

**#forcol** is used for creating data for multiple columns (see the figure below). This statement can be used in conjunction with the **#forrow** statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The forcol directive.

The following figure demonstrates the output of the above code.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from the figure above.

#### Displaying content in a cell

Texts in any generated report are always wrapped. Also, the cells’ width in the generated report depends on the cells’ width in the report template used. For example, an *XLSX* report template in the first figure below will generate an output report as shown in the second figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The forrow directive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from the figure above.

#### Limitation when used in Microsoft Office Excel worksheet

You cannot use **#sectionBegin** and **#includeSection** in *XLSX*. If you try to use **#sectionBegin** or **#includeSection** in an *XLSX* report template (the first figure below) an error message will open (the second figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Invalid usage of a sectionBegin directive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The error message from running the code in the figure above.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2>Multi-Line Statements in <em>XLSX</em></h2><p>All multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions.</p><p style="text-align: left;">The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single cell. The figures below show samples of invalid usage of the <strong>#if</strong> and <strong>#foreach</strong> statements between cells respectively.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_6.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Executing multi-line statements.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_7.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid usage of #foreach statements.</h6><p>In the first figure, since the body of the #if statement ($e.name) resides in the A2 cell, not in the A1 cell, the body will not be generated when generating a report, regardless of the evaluation of the #if statement. The code shown in the second figure will break the structure of a spreadsheet document.</p><p style="text-align: left;">The following two figures demonstrate samples of valid usage of the #if and #foreach statements respectively.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_8.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Valid usage of an #if statement.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_9.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Valid usage of a #foreach statement.</h6><ul><li>A VTL Macro must be declared within a single cell. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single cell, see figure below.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_10.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid usage of a VTL Macro.</h6><p>The macro will copy all contents between #macro and #end. Cells and rows will be included in the macro as well. Once this record has been inserted, the macro content will break the document structure.</p><p>The following figure demonstrates a sample of valid usage of the #macro statement.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_11.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Valid usage of a VTL Macro.</h6><h2>Creating data for multiple rows</h2><p style="text-align: left;">The <strong>#foreach</strong> directive can only be used in a single cell record. To create data for multiple rows, use the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive instead (see the following figure).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_12.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The forrow directive.</h6><p style="text-align: left;">The figure below demonstrates the output of the above code.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_13.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from figure 7.</h6><h2>Creating data for multiple columns</h2><p><strong>#forcol</strong> is used for creating data for multiple columns (see the figure below). This statement can be used in conjunction with the <strong>#forrow</strong> statement.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_14.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The forcol directive.</h6><p>The following figure demonstrates the output of the above code.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_15.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from the figure above.</h6><h2>Displaying content in a cell</h2><p>Texts in any generated report are always wrapped. Also, the cells’ width in the generated report depends on the cells’ width in the report template used. For example, an <em>XLSX</em> report template in the first figure below will generate an output report as shown in the second figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Wrapped Text (Column B) in an XLSX Report Template" ac:alt="Sample of Wrapped Text (Column B) in an XLSX Report Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_16.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The forrow directive.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Wrapping Text Output in XLSX" ac:alt="Wrapping Text Output in XLSX"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_17.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from the figure above.</h6><h2>Limitation when used in Microsoft Office Excel worksheet</h2><p>You cannot use <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#includeSection</strong> in <em>XLSX</em>. If you try to use <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> or <strong>#includeSection</strong> in an <em>XLSX</em> report template (the first figure below) an error message will open (the second figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_18.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid usage of a sectionBegin directive.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 14- The error message from running the code in figure 13..png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Excel worksheet (XLSX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The error message from running the code in the figure above.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999693 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01117: (2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)

- page_id: `303999693`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999693/2026x+Refresh1+Microsoft+Office+PowerPoint+presentation+PPTX
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:49.335+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix B: office open XML format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A presentation document requires a special document template. This template does not contain any content order, and all text content is always placed inside a text box. A text box is an image structure (an image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates). You can change a Text box position. You can also place a Text box in the same positions as others (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of PPTX template.

#### Multi-line statements in PPTX

Similar to *XLSX*, all multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions.

1. The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single text box. The following code shows a sample of invalid usage of the#foreach statement between the text boxes. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Since the PPTX template does not provide the statement order, the template will not be interpreted as the order of the displayed images (text boxes). For example, $uc.name may not be processed after #foreach($uc in $UseCase) has been completely processed. The following code demonstrates a sample of valid usage of the #foreach statement. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. A VTL Macro must be declared within a single text box. Do not insert the multi-cell recorded macros in a single text box. See the following code example. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Since each text box does not have any sequence order, the macro cannot record any content between the text boxes. The following code example demonstrates a valid usage of the #macro statement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Creating data for multiple slides

In PPTX report templates, you can use the #forpage directive to create additional slide(s) in your presentation. You can use #forpage and #endpage directives in any text box. However, the #endpage directive must be on same slide as the #forpage directive or on the following slide, but not before the slide which contains the #forpage directive. All directives on the slide(s) between 1) the slide the #forpage directive appears and 2) the slide the #endpage directive appears will be included within the #forpage statement. For example, the template in the first figure below will produce the output as shown in the second figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating data for multiple pages using the forpage directive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Results of running the code from the figure above.

#### Creating a page with conditions

Since a directive in the *PPTX* report template does not provide any statement order (unless residing in the same text box), you cannot used the #if directive together with the #forpage directive. See figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The code above will not produce the output report as expected, because the #forpage directive automatically covers all directives in the current page regardless of the statement order. Consequently, the #if directive may not be interpreted after the #forpage directive.

To avoid this problem, you can use the $report.filterElement($elements, $types) method. This helper method provides the element filter for the specified type. In this case, use the following code.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more details on $report.filterElement($elements, $types), see Section *[Helper Modules](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Helper+tools)*.

#### Limitation when used in Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation

**#sectionBegin**, **#includeSection** cannot be used in any *PPTX* report template. If you try, for example, to use **#sectionBegin** in a *PPTX* report template, an error message (the figure below) will open.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The error message dialog that appears from running an invalid sectionBegin directive.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A presentation document requires a special document template. This template does not contain any content order, and all text content is always placed inside a text box. A text box is an image structure (an image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates). You can change a Text box position. You can also place a Text box in the same positions as others (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of PPTX Template" ac:alt="Sample of PPTX Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_20.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">A sample of PPTX template.</h6><h2>Multi-line statements in PPTX</h2><p>Similar to <em>XLSX</em>, all multi-line directives such as #if, #else, #elseif, #foreach, and #macro must be used under the following conditions.</p><ol><li><p>The beginning and ending statements must be declared within a single text box. The following code shows a sample of invalid usage of the#foreach statement between the text boxes.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_21.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adb06cf4-2d0c-48ff-a8d3-82434924a373"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Since the PPTX template does not provide the statement order, the template will not be interpreted as the order of the displayed images (text boxes). For example, $uc.name may not be processed after #foreach($uc in $UseCase) has been completely processed. The following code demonstrates a sample of valid usage of the #foreach statement.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_22.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>A VTL Macro must be declared within a single text box. See the following code example.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_23.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p>Since each text box does not have any sequence order, the macro cannot record any content between the text boxes. The following code example demonstrates a valid usage of the #macro statement.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_24.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Creating data for multiple slides</h2><p>In PPTX report templates, you can use the #forpage directive to create additional slide(s) in your presentation. You can use #forpage and #endpage directives in any text box. However, the #endpage directive must be on same slide as the #forpage directive or on the following slide, but not before the slide which contains the #forpage directive. All directives on the slide(s) between 1) the slide the #forpage directive appears and 2) the slide the #endpage directive appears will be included within the #forpage statement. For example, the template in the first figure below will produce the output as shown in the second figure below.<br /> </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_25.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Creating data for multiple pages using the forpage directive.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_26.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Results of running the code from the figure above.</h6><h2>Creating a page with conditions</h2><p>Since a directive in the <em>PPTX</em> report template does not provide any statement order (unless residing in the same text box), you cannot used the #if directive together with the #forpage directive. See figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Looping With Condition in General Style" ac:alt="Sample of Looping With Condition in General Style"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_27.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The code above will not produce the output report as expected, because the #forpage directive automatically covers all directives in the current page regardless of the statement order. Consequently, the #if directive may not be interpreted after the #forpage directive.</p><p>To avoid this problem, you can use the $report.filterElement($elements, $types) method. This helper method provides the element filter for the specified type. In this case, use the following code.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Looping With Conditional Filter" ac:alt="Sample of Looping With Conditional Filter"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_28.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>For more details on $report.filterElement($elements, $types), see Section <em><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Helper+tools">Helper Modules</a></em>.</p><h2>Limitation when used in Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation</h2><p><strong>#sectionBegin</strong>, <strong>#includeSection</strong> cannot be used in any <em>PPTX</em> report template. If you try, for example, to use <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> in a <em>PPTX</em> report template, an error message (the figure below) will open.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_29.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"> </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 4- The error message dialog that appears from running an invalid sectionBegin directive..png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The error message dialog that appears from running an invalid sectionBegin directive.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999560 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01118: (2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)

- page_id: `303999560`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999560/2026x+Refresh1+Microsoft+Office+Word+document+DOCX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:07.589+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix B: office open XML format template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports most *DOCX* features. You can place the *VTL* codes inside core (properties) and content of any *DOCX* file. All syntax usable in *RTF* can also be used in *DOCX*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document.

#### Limitations when used in Microsoft Office Word document

You cannot use multi-line statements in different objects. If you try to use them in*DOCX*, an error message will open. See the following example.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Error Message of Invalid Usage of the Multi-line Statements in DOCX.

#### Creating data for multiple columns

**#forcol** is used for creating data for multiple columns in a row. This statement must be defined in table and can be used in conjunction with the **#forrow** statement. For example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Sample elements in the Containment tree.

**Template Code:**

| Name | #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**#forcol** may create different number of columns in each row. See the example below.

**Template Code:**

| #forrow($p in $sPackage) $p.name | #forcol($o in $sorter.sort($p.ownedElement))$o.name#endcol#endrow |
| --- | --- |

**Output:**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**#forcol** must be defined as the first statement in a column because it is parsed and processed before other statements in the column except **#forrow**. See the example below.

**Template Code**

| Name | #set($p = $Package) #forcol($ap in $p)$ap.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**Above code will be parsed to**

| Name | #forcol($ap in $p) #set($p = $Package)$ap.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

So, the template code should be:

Template Code:

| Name#set($p = $Package) | #forcol($ap in $p) $ap.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**#forcol** will create dynamic columns in a row. This directive does not create columns for the whole table. See the example below.

**Template Code:**

| Name | #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Docx supports at most 63 columns per row (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Docx supports at most 63 columns per row.

#### Creating merged column horizontally for DOCX

ReportWizard provides utility functions for creating table properties. The functions of this module are accessible from templates through **$tableprop**. In this version, ReportWizard provides only **$tableprop.mergeColumns()** to create merged columns horizontally in *DOCX* template.

This statement must be defined in table.

****

**1. $tableprop.mergeColumns(int number)**

Merging columns in specified number

Where the parameter is:

- number - the number of columns to merge. This value has to be of type integer and start from 2.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns($Package.size())Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

**2. $tableprop.mergeColumns(String stringNumber)**

Merging columns in specified number as String

Where the parameter is:

- stringNumber - the string value of number of columns to be merged. This value has to be of type integer string and start from “2”.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns(“3”) Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**$table.mergeColumns()** must be defined as the first statement in the column because it is parsed and processed before other statements in column expect **#forrow** and **#forcol.** See the example below.

****

**Template Code:**

| Project | #set($gValue = $Package.size() +1)$tableprop.mergeColumns($gValue)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package) $p.name#endcol | No Package |

Above code will be parsed to

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns($gValue)#set($gValue = $Package.size() + 1)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package) $p.name#endcol | No Package |

**Output:**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

So, the template code should be:

**Template Code:**

| Project#set($gValue = $Package.size() + 1) | $tableprop.mergeColumns($gValue)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol | No Package |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**$tableprop.mergecolumns()** will make a column to be a merged column, it does not change the number of columns in a row. See the following example.

****

**Template Code:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns(“5”)Packages |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |  |

**Output:**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The number of columns in the first row is still 3.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard supports most <em>DOCX</em> features. You can place the <em>VTL</em> codes inside core (properties) and content of any <em>DOCX</em> file. All syntax usable in <em>RTF</em> can also be used in <em>DOCX</em>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document" ac:alt="A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_1.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">A Sample of DOCX Converted from an RTF Document.</h6><h2>Limitations when used in Microsoft Office Word document</h2><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You cannot use multi-line statements in different objects. If you try to use them in </span><em>DOCX</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, an error message will open. See the following example.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Invalid Usage of Multi-line Statement in DOCX" ac:alt="Invalid Usage of Multi-line Statement in DOCX"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ooxml_4.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_export.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Error Message of Invalid Usage of the Multi-line Statements in DOCX.</h6><h2>Creating data for multiple columns</h2><p><strong>#forcol</strong> is used for creating data for multiple columns in a row. This statement must be defined in table and can be used in conjunction with the <strong>#forrow</strong> statement. For example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Sample elements in MagicDraw Containment Tree" ac:alt="Sample elements in MagicDraw Containment Tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Sample elements in the Containment tree.</h6><p><strong>Template Code:</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><pre>Name</pre></td><td><pre> #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b228f395-e33f-4df1-891f-3c06c3e58988"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>#forcol</strong> may create different number of columns in each row. See the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Template Code:</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>#forrow($<span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);">p</span> in $sPackage) $p.name</td><td>#forcol($o in $sorter.sort($p.ownedElement))<br />$o.name#endcol#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Output:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66ec4a0d-2a3a-4106-af12-75e51f6dc928"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>#forcol</strong> must be defined as the first statement in a column because it is parsed and processed before other statements in the column except <strong>#forrow</strong>. See the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Template Code</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>Name</td><td>#set($p = $Package) #forcol($ap in $p)<br />$ap.name#endcol</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Above code will be parsed to </strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>Name</td><td>#forcol($ap in $p) #set($p = $Package)<br />$ap.name#endcol</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Output:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>So, the template code should be:</p><p>Template Code:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td>Name#set($p = $Package)</td><td>#forcol($ap in $p) $ap.name#endcol</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Output:</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="forrow-5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Microsoft Office Word document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40a9cd37-ed82-4ed0-b604-1844c3293d67"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>#forcol</strong> will create dynamic columns in a row. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998352 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01119: (2026x Refresh1) Migrating BPMN2 server projects

- page_id: `303998352`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998352/2026x+Refresh1+Migrating+BPMN2+server+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:30.658+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN2 project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project.

To migrate a server project

1. In the Model Browser, open the **Locked Elements** tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users. If elements are locked by other users, each user must unlock their own locked elements.Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.
2. Perform the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Projects Migration' space=''] .
3. Commit the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project.</p><p><br /></p><p>To migrate a server project</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="37f779da-8593-4c9f-b26f-d7a20c0cee06"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Model Browser, open the <strong>Locked Elements</strong> tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db086d4-c5c7-4f6c-8fcb-68f3472a4ca6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If elements are locked by other users, each user must unlock their own locked elements.</p><p>Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="f19d67d9-82b6-4f1f-9371-65f4137b2f81">Perform the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Projects Migration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project migration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="2aafb69d-c586-412e-a6e9-61ad7dbfa572">Commit the project.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999402 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01120: (2026x Refresh1) Migrating from BPMN 1.1

- page_id: `303999402`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999402/2026x+Refresh1+Migrating+from+BPMN+1.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:52.754+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > (2026x Refresh1) Migration
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The MagicDraw 16.8 and earlier versions support BPMN 1.1 and Business Process diagrams, this let you to model business processes using the BPMN 1.1 notation.

The support for BPMN 1.1 and Business Process diagrams is no longer available from MagicDraw version 17.0.1. It is replaced by the Cameo Business Modeler support for the BPMN 2.0 specification.

You can convert your MagicDraw projects containing old BPMN profile and Business Process diagrams to the new BPMN 2.0 standard. A backup file will be created for the project conversion. This backup file lets you find your BPMN 1.1 models when you need them.

To convert a BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0 by opening it

1. Open a MagicDraw project that contains a BPMN Profile or Business Process diagram. A Question dialog opens.
2. Click Yes to convert the project.
3. Click OK .

To convert a BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0 by importing it

1. On the main menu, click File > Import From > BPMN 1.1 Project . The Open BPMN 1.1 Project dialog opens.
2. Select a project that contains a Business Process diagram and click Open .
3. Click OK .

A backup file project_name_bpmn1.1.bak is created when the project has been migrated.The file includes the BPMN 1.1 project before the migration.

To convert a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0

Before migrating a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0, make sure that other users have not locked the BPMN elements.

1. On the main menu, click File > Import From > BPMN 1.1 Project . A Question dialog opens.
2. Click Yes to convert the project. Another Question dialog opens to inform you that all elements will be locked.
3. Click Yes .

A backup file for a Teamwork project will not be created when migrating the project to BPMN 2.0. Use a Teamwork version as a backup copy that has included the BPMN 1.1 Business Process diagrams before migrating the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It is replaced by the Cameo Business Modeler support for the BPMN 2.0 specification.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can convert your MagicDraw projects containing old BPMN profile and Business Process diagrams to the new BPMN 2.0 standard. A backup file will be created for the project conversion. A Question dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to convert the project.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To convert a BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0 by importing it</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>BPMN 1.1 Project</strong>. The <strong>Open BPMN 1.1 Project</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select a project that contains a Business Process diagram and click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d199a91a-85a8-45af-bef3-64c4e7aedeca"><ac:rich-text-body>A backup file project_name_bpmn1.1.bak is created when the project has been migrated.The file includes the BPMN 1.1 project before the migration.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To convert a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82d243a8-680b-468b-b2e1-03a123601b91"><ac:rich-text-body>Before migrating a Teamwork BPMN 1.1 project to BPMN 2.0, make sure that other users have not locked the BPMN elements.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>BPMN 1.1 Project</strong>. Use a Teamwork version as a backup copy that has included the BPMN 1.1 Business Process diagrams before migrating the project.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000434 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01121: (2026x Refresh1) Migrating Server Projects

- page_id: `304000434`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000434/2026x+Refresh1+Migrating+Server+Projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:09.892+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.

To migrate a server project

1. In the Model Browser, open the **Locked Elements** tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users. If elements are locked by other users, each user must unlock their own locked elements.Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.
2. Perform the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Projects Migration' space=''] .
3. Commit the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.</p><p><br /></p><p>To migrate a server project</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Model Browser, open the <strong>Locked Elements</strong> tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db086d4-c5c7-4f6c-8fcb-68f3472a4ca6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If elements are locked by other users, each user must unlock their own locked elements.</p><p>Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Perform the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Projects Migration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project migration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Commit the project.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989866 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01122: (2026x Refresh1) Migrating to UML Profile for BPMN2 v1.0

- page_id: `303989866`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989866/2026x+Refresh1+Migrating+to+UML+Profile+for+BPMN2+v1.0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:18:23.857+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > (2026x Refresh1) Migration
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The BPMN2 is supported by Cameo Business Modeler Plugin 17.0 and later versions of the tool; however, some further changes have been made in Cameo Business Modeler Plugin 19.0 to improve the UML Profile for BPMN2 v1.0 support. The migration comes with the changes listed below:

- Some BPMN elements have been renamed

| Old Name | New Name |
| --- | --- |
| Message | BPMNMessage |
| Relationship | BPMNRelationship |
| BPMNImport | Import |
| Operation | BPMNOperation |
| Association | BPMNAssociation |
| EventGatewayType | EventBasedGatewayType |
| Event | BPMNEvent |

- Some Stereotypes' metaclasses have been changed

| Stereotype | Old Metaclass Name | New Metaclass Name |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Resource | Actor, Class | Class |
| PartnerRole | Actor, Class | Class |
| ReceiveTask | OpaqueAction | AcceptEventAction |
| SendTask | OpaqueAction | CallOperationAction |
| ServiceTask | OpaqueAction | CallOperationAction |
| Task | OpaqueAction | Action |
| BPMNMessage | CentralBufferNode | Class |
| Message Flow | Dependency | Information Flow |

Please check your model if you use Stereotypes for BPMN elements. They could have been affected by the migration.

- Some elements have been removed from the BPMN Profile

| Removed Element |
| --- |
| MessageFlowNode |
| Correlation |
| AttributeProperty |
| PinProperty |
| VariableProperty |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000415 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01126: (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.3

- page_id: `304000415`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000415/2026x+Refresh1+Migration+from+UAF+1.0+to+UAF+1.3
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:07.902+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is **NO DIRECT MIGRATION**from **UAF 1.0** (last supported modeling tool version - **19.0**) to **UAF 1.3** (first supported modeling tool version - **2026x**).

##### How to migrate

To be able to migrate your **UAF 1.0** project, you need to perform the following steps:​

1. Open your UAF 1.0 project with the modeling tool 2022x or 2024x version, and migrate it to UAF 1.2 . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2' space='']>>]]>
2. Open the **UAF 1.2** project with the modeling tool **2026x** version and migrate it to **UAF 1.3**.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3' space='']>>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">There is <strong>NO DIRECT MIGRATION </strong>from <strong>UAF 1.0</strong> (last supported modeling tool version - <strong>19.0</strong>) to <strong>UAF 1.3</strong> (first supported modeling tool version - <strong>2026x</strong>).</span></p><h3>How to migrate</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><span>To be able to migrate your <strong>UAF 1.0</strong> project, you need to perform the following steps:</span><span>​</span></p><ol><li data-uuid="8be92791-9523-4c84-a8e9-aaf99dcb04b0">Open your <strong>UAF 1.0</strong> project with the modeling tool <strong>2022x</strong> or <strong>2024x</strong> version, and migrate it to <strong>UAF 1.2</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="b2fac427-b84d-4beb-a53f-e52ad54f5e33"><span>Open the <strong>UAF 1.2</strong> project with the modeling tool <strong>2026x</strong> version and migrate it to <strong>UAF 1.3</strong>.<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.2 to UAF 1.3" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ol>
````

The duration of the projects migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2 depends on the project sizes. In the following chart, you can see the duration of local and Teamwork Cloud projects migration:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For the recommendations of RAM for the projects migration, see [Installation, licensing, and system requirements](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&version=2026x Refresh1).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996780 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01131: (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1

- page_id: `303996780`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996780/2026x+Refresh1+Migration+from+UPDM+3+UAF+1.0+beta+to+UAF+1.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:21.482+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:

- 
- 

##### Migrating to UAF 1.0

To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0

1. Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.
2. Save the project.

For more information about changes, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0' space='']

##### Migrating to UAF 1.1

After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.0"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[First, you need to migrate your project to UAF 1.0. ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Then you can migrate that project to UAF 1.1.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.0</h3><p>To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6acb6dfe-1750-475f-bab8-7fe3ff6c30d2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Save the project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information about changes, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0" /></ac:link></p><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.1</h3><p>After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1" /></ac:link></p>
````

- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:18:35.853+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDminExclusive. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: minExclusive XML representation summary

minExclusive XML representation summary

```text
<minExclusive
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = anySimpleType
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</minExclusive>
```

```text
{value} must be in the value space of {base type definition}.
```

#### PANEL: minExclusive XML code sample

minExclusive XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which limits values to integers greater than or equal to 100, using minExclusive.
```

```text
<simpleType name= 'more- than-ninety-nine'>
```

```text
<restriction base= 'integer'>		<minExclusive value= '99'/>	</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType>
```

```text
Note that the value space of this datatype is identical to the previous one (named 'one-­hundred-or-more').
```

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDminLength. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: minLength XML representation summary

minLength XML representation summary

```text
<minLength
```

```text
fixed = Boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = nonNegativeInteger
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}› 	Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</minLength>
```

#### PANEL: minLength XML code sample

minLength XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which requires strings to have at least one character (i.e., the empty string is not in the value space of this datatype).
```

```text
<simpleType name='non-empty-string'›
```

```text
<restriction base='string'>
```

```text
<minLength value='1'/>
```

```text
</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType›
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999196 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01136: (2026x Refresh1) Missing data in diagrams, matrices, tables, and relation maps

- page_id: `303999196`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999196/2026x+Refresh1+Missing+data+in+diagrams+matrices+tables+and+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:40.366+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > (2026x Refresh1) Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Since UPDM 2 profile [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Removed elements' space=''], the Element Type of matrices, tables, and relation maps becomes empty after the migration. The same goes for the customized elements derived from the removed UPDM2 elements. If the UPDM2 [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Replaced and renamed elements' space=''] to the UAF element, the UAF element is set as an Element Type (criteria).

#### NOTE: Exception

Exception

If Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, Service Interaction was set as an Element Type criteria, after the migration an Interaction replaces those elements.

After the migration from UPDM 2 to UAF, some tags are no longer supported. During the migration process, all the information from those tags is moved to the**To Do**property of the tag owner. To find this property, open the appropriate element's Specification window. You can display the tag information in the note, anchored to the element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Since UPDM 2 profile <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Removed elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elements were removed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the Element Type of matrices, tables, and relation maps becomes empty after the migration. The same goes for the customized elements derived from the removed UPDM2 elements. If the UPDM2 <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Replaced and renamed elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element is migrated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the UAF element, the UAF element is set as an Element Type (criteria).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f830871-f7d3-43f9-a110-feeded1958ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Exception</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If Operational Event Trace, <span>Resource Event Trace, <span>Service Interaction was set as an Element Type criteria, after the migration an Interaction replaces those elements.</span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After the migration from UPDM 2 to UAF, some tags are no longer supported. You can display the tag information in the note, anchored to the element.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_tagged_values.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Missing data in diagrams, matrices, tables, and relation maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3>Removed tag added to ToDo property</h3><p>Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993827 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01138: (2026x Refresh1) Missing expression language

- page_id: `303993827`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993827/2026x+Refresh1+Missing+expression+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:40.294+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine > (2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tool supports multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages' space=''] you can use for expression specification. Additionally, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages' space=''] you may need. However, if your project contains expressions that use a scripting language that is neither built-in nor manually added to the tool, it is considered missing in the tool; thus, the specified expressions cannot be executed, causing errors in the project.

##### Expression evaluation failure

For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed. Since, in this case, JavaScript Nashorn is neither built-in nor added manually to the modeling tool, the script cannot be executed, causing an evaluation error, as shown below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The error notification indicates the element containing the failed expression. Clicking the icon on the bottom right opens a Notification Window that displays the element name with the script that cannot be executed.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Expression evaluation failure notification in the Notification Window

The missing language is also indicated in the Expression dialog's Language field for scripts.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Missing language indication in the Expression dialog

##### Resolving expression evaluation failure

To resolve an expression evaluation failure, do one of the following

- Rewrite the script in a scripting language that is already supported by the modeling tool. To learn more about the built-in languages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages' space=''] page.
- Add the missing language to the modeling tool. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The modeling tool supports multiple <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[built-in scripting languages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you can use for expression specification. Additionally, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[add other scripting languages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you may need. However, if your project contains expressions that use a scripting language that is neither built-in nor manually added to the tool, it is considered missing in the tool; thus, the specified expressions cannot be executed, causing errors in the project.</p><h3>Expression evaluation failure</h3><p>For example, if you open a table containing a custom column that has an expression with a JavaScript Nashorn script, an 'Expression evaluation failure' error notification is displayed. Since, in this case, JavaScript Nashorn is neither built-in nor added manually to the modeling tool, the script cannot be executed, causing an evaluation error, as shown below. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expression_evaluation_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Missing expression language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </h6><p>The error notification indicates the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">element containing the failed expression</span>. Clicking the icon on the bottom right opens a Notification Window that displays the element name with the script that cannot be executed. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="notification_window_expression_evaluation_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Missing expression language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Expression evaluation failure notification in the Notification Window</h6><p>The missing language is also indicated in the Expression dialog's Language field for scripts.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="missing_language_expression_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Missing expression language" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Missing language indication in the Expression dialog</h6><h3>Resolving expression evaluation failure</h3><p>To resolve an expression evaluation failure, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Rewrite the script in a scripting language that is already supported by the modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ea17ebc-15ef-460f-b0f5-cbbe38882859"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about the built-in languages, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Add the missing language to the modeling tool. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de6f0ee7-9c62-4a58-b0b8-c4c1f29387c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool" /></ac:link> page. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul>

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487246060

1487246062

1487246061

**Description**

A Mission indicates the ongoing operational Activity of the enterprise. The Mission describes what the business is or will be doing on a day-to-day basis.

A Mission makes a Vision operative. It indicates the ongoing Activity that makes the Vision a reality. A Mission is planned by means of Strategies.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487246059

**Related elements**

- Means Concepts
- Mean Concept Relationships

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram
