<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995350 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01197: (2026x Refresh1) Model Merge

- page_id: `303995350`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995350/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:28.095+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['model-merge', 'concepts', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1994020602

INLINE

1994020605

INLINE

1994020604

INLINE

You need to have the Project Merge plugin installed to use the merge functionality.

Model Merge allows copying changes between different project versions. This functionality is usually needed when there are several branches that reflect different releases or versions of the product, for example, when certain fixes have to be copied from a release branch to the mainstream development

You can merge different versions of both local and server projects.

Projects that use other projects can be compared and merged in one single operation. You do not need to merge each used project and finally the main project one-by-one.

##### Basic concepts

For a better understanding of the further material, learn the following concepts.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SourceSource | A project version from which changes are copied. |
| TargetTarget | A project version into which changes are copied. |
| Ancestor | A common parent project version of both source and target. |
| Contributor | General description for a project version that participates in the merge as a source or a target. |

1994020601

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995555 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01199: (2026x Refresh1) Model Obfuscation

- page_id: `303995555`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995555/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Obfuscation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:45.332+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to have the Model Obfuscator plugin installed to use the obfuscation functionality.

Model Obfuscator plugin is used to obfuscate confidential data (texts or images) in the local project for sending it to No Magic support when reporting related issue. The obfuscation process changes user project data strings to random strings (e.g. class name "User_login" will be changed to "xcolnyrwxx") and saves it as a new project.

##### Data obfuscation

To perform data obfuscation

1. In the main menu, click File > Obfuscate Project . The Save dialog opens.
2. Define the name and select the location in your file system for the obfuscated project. Click Save .
3. Once the obfuscated project is saved, the message "Obfuscation process is done" is displayed. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='model_obfuscation.png']

###### An example of a project with obfuscated data.

##### Custom data obfuscation

You can create your own custom encryption algorithm instead of using the default one.

To create a custom obfuscation algorithm

1. Create a new plugin as described on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Writing plugins' space=''] page. 1.1. You can compile your plugin's main class by defining *<install.root>/lib/md_api.jar* in the classpath parameter. 
1.2. Create an additional class that extends *com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelobfuscator.WordObfuscator* in your plugin. For an example, see the code below: java
2. Compile it and include this class in your plugin's jar. When compiling the class, define <install.root>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelobfuscator/modelobfuscator_api.jar in the classpath parameters.
3. Add the Java option -Dmo_word_obfuscator_impl\=<full class name> (e.g. -Dmo_word_obfuscator_impl\=myplugin.SecureWordObfuscator ) in the application's properties file (e.g. magidraw.properties ) at the end of the JAVA_ARGS= line.
4. Open the modeling tool and obfuscate your project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5262e269-d260-45a2-b63e-a95ee7c60697"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the Model Obfuscator plugin installed to use the obfuscation functionality.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Model Obfuscator plugin is used to obfuscate confidential data (texts or images) in the local project for sending it to No Magic support when reporting related issue. The obfuscation process changes user project data strings to random strings (e.g. class name &quot;User_login&quot; will be changed to &quot;xcolnyrwxx&quot;) and saves it as a new project.</p><h3>Data obfuscation</h3><p>To perform data obfuscation </p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Obfuscate Project</strong>. The <strong>Save</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Define the name and select the location in your file system for the obfuscated project. Click <strong>Save</strong>.</li><li>Once the obfuscated project is saved, the message &quot;Obfuscation process is done&quot; is displayed. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_obfuscation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Obfuscation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of a project with obfuscated data.</h6><h3>Custom data obfuscation</h3><p>You can create your own custom encryption algorithm instead of using the default one.</p><p>To create a custom obfuscation algorithm</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a new plugin as described on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Writing plugins" /></ac:link> page. <br /><p>1.1. You can compile your plugin's main class by defining <em>&lt;install.root&gt;/lib/md_api.jar</em> in the classpath parameter.<br />1.2. Create an additional class that extends <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelobfuscator.WordObfuscator</em> in your plugin. For an example, see the code below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9e7095c-c2b9-40e2-8c28-465320880bfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package myplugin;

import com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelobfuscator.WordObfuscator;
import java.security.SecureRandom;

public class SecureWordObfuscator implements WordObfuscator
{
	private static final char STARTING_CHAR = 97;
	private static final int BOUND = 26;

	private final SecureRandom random = new SecureRandom();

	@Override
	public String obfuscateWord(String word)
	{
		int len = word.length();
		StringBuilder tokenBuilder = new StringBuilder(len);
		for (int i = 0; i < len; i++)
		{
			tokenBuilder.append(String.valueOf(Character.toChars(STARTING_CHAR + (char) random.nextInt(BOUND))));
		}
		System.out.println("Original word: " + word  + " obfuscated word: " + tokenBuilder.toString());
		return tokenBuilder.toString();
	}
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Compile it and include this class in your plugin's jar. When compiling the class, define <em>&lt;install.root&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelobfuscator/modelobfuscator_api.jar</em> in the classpath parameters.</li><li>Add the Java option <em>-Dmo_word_obfuscator_impl\=&lt;full class name&gt;</em> (e.g. <em>-Dmo_word_obfuscator_impl\=myplugin.SecureWordObfuscator</em>) in the application's properties file (e.g. <em>magidraw.properties</em>) at the end of the <em>JAVA_ARGS= </em>line.</li><li>Open the modeling tool and obfuscate your project.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995519 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01200: (2026x Refresh1) Model Patch

- page_id: `303995519`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995519/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Patch
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:42.276+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model Patchtechnology allows you to take a part of a model (e.g., one package), export it to a patch file, and then bring that part of the model into another project or branch.

You can use the Model Patch mechanism:

- As an alternative for model merge when the source of truth is stored in the source version.
- For disconnected team collaboration when changes are made only in a certain scope, and it is not necessary to bring the whole project.
- To bring only necessary parts into the model rather than an entire used project.

To learn about the different types of model patches and how to use them, see:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="">Model Patch<span> </span>technology allows you to take a part of a model (e.g., one package), export it to a patch file, and then bring that part of the model into another project or branch.</p><p style="">You can use the Model Patch mechanism:</p><ul style=""><li>As an alternative for model merge when the source of truth is stored in the source version.</li><li>For disconnected team collaboration when changes are made only in a certain scope, and it is not necessary to bring the whole project.</li><li>To bring only necessary parts into the model rather than an entire used project.</li></ul><p style="">To learn about the different types of model patches and how to use them, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e1047f72-8459-4182-a39b-8a0cfc48d3dc" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997525 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01201: (2026x Refresh1) Model Patch types

- page_id: `303997525`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997525/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Patch+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:18.222+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Patch
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

When creating a model patch, you have two options to choose from: **All selected content**and **Changes from selected version**:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a model patch type.

This article explains the difference between the two options and when to use each one.

##### All selected content

Model patches of the type *All selected content*can be created from and applied to local, Teamwork Cloud, and **3D**EXPERIENCE projects.

Using this patch type, you can bring whole selected elements as they are. This patch type is always considered to be the source of truth, meaning that the patch content overrides your existing elements and/or adds elements that exist in the source version but not in the target.

For example, your source model contains Package A with Class A and Class B, while your target model contains Package A with Class C and Class D. You decide to apply a model patch with Package A containing Class A and Class B. Since the patch is considered the source of truth, Class A and Class B are added to the target model, while Class C and Class D are deleted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying a model patch of the *All selected content* type.

##### Changes from the selected version

You can only create a model patch of the **Changes from selected version** type from a Teamwork Cloud project. However, you can apply this type of model patch to any project.

If you do not need to override existing elements in the target model but want to add them as concurrent changes, you can choose to bring changes from a specific version.

Example process:

1. Y ou have a *Requirements*package in your project trunk. Y ou create a new project branch with 3 new requirements: 1, 2, and 3 in the *Requirements*package.
2. Meanwhile, another user creates requirement 4 in the package *Requirements*of the project trunk.
3. You choose to export the Requirements package as a patch from a specific version (the branch version). Therefore, the patch contains only the requirements you created: 1, 2, and 3.
4. You apply the patch to the project trunk, which now contains requirement 4 in its Requirements package. Since you only extracted changes, the patch does not override existing elements; requirements 1, 2, and 3 are created without deleting requirement 4.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying a model patch of the *Changes from selected version* type.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ffde229-4855-49e8-8672-122e7e2b2935"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>When creating a model patch, you have two options to choose from: <strong>All selected content </strong>and <strong>Changes from selected version</strong>:</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="patch_types.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Patch types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting a model patch type.</h6><p>This article explains the difference between the two options and when to use each one.</p><h3>All selected content</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c883924-9107-43da-af1e-554ccb6e0234"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Model patches of the type <em>All selected content </em>can be created from and applied to local, Teamwork Cloud, and <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Using this patch type, you can bring whole selected elements as they are. This patch type is always considered to be the source of truth, meaning that the patch content overrides your existing elements and/or adds elements that exist in the source version but not in the target.</span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For example, your source model contains Package A with Class A and Class B, while your target model contains Package A with Class C and Class D. You decide to apply a model patch with Package A containing Class A and Class B. Since the patch is considered the source of truth, Class A and Class B are added to the target model, while Class C and Class D are deleted.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_patch_all_selected_content.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Patch types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying a model patch of the <em>All selected content</em> type.</h6><h3>Changes from the selected version</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="281f347a-3092-4dc4-ac62-ff660a118565"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can only create a model patch of the <strong>Changes from selected version</strong> type from a Teamwork Cloud project. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">However, you can apply this type of model patch to any project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you do not need to override existing elements in the target model but want to add them as concurrent changes, you can choose to bring changes from a specific version.</p><p>Example process:</p><ol><li>Y<span>ou have a <em>Requirements </em>package in your project trunk. Y</span><span>ou create a new project branch with 3 new requirements: 1, 2, and 3 in the <em>Requirements </em>package.</span></li><li><span>Meanwhile, another user creates requirement 4 in the package <em>Requirements </em>of the project trunk. </span></li><li>You choose to export the <em>Requirements </em>package as a patch from a specific version (the branch version). Therefore, the patch contains only the requirements you created: 1, 2, and 3.</li><li>You apply the patch to the project trunk, which now contains requirement 4 in its <em>Requirements </em>package. Since you only extracted changes, the patch does not override existing elements; requirements 1, 2, and 3 are created without deleting requirement 4.</li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_patch_chnages_from_specific_version.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Patch types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying a model patch of the <em>Changes from selected version</em> type.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995449 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01202: (2026x Refresh1) Model Transformation Wizard

- page_id: `303995449`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995449/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Transformation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:35.135+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformation engine itself is available in the modeling tool editions from Standard and up. 
However, only Architect and Enterprise editions bring any particular transformations. So for Standard and Professional edition users, transformations are not available.

Plugins can bring additional transformations regardless of the edition. For example, users, who have the Cameo Data Modeler plugin, can use the transformation engine to run ER to SQL(Generic/ Oracle) transformations, even if they do not have the Architect/Enterprise editions.

The Model Transformation Wizard enables running one of the predefined transformations. When using this wizard to run a chosen transformation, you have to perform the following steps:

1. Choose a transformation type.
2. Specify both the transformation source model (or a part of it) and destination package.
3. Select a type map.
4. Set custom transformation properties.

Each transformation converts the chosen model part into another model according to the rules of this transformation. Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains, e.g., UML and SQL.

All transformations follow a similar approach. They take a part of a model as the transformation source and copy it to the destination model, establishing traces between the transformation source and target elements. Then each transformation performs the specific model reorganizations, which are necessary for each 
transformation type according to the transformation options specified by the user in the transformation wizard. Transformation can also be performed in-place, i.e., the source model is not copied to the destination model, but transformation works directly on it instead.

Transformations also perform the so-called type remapping. During the transformation between the different modeling domains, such as UML and SQL, it is necessary to go through the data types used in the source model and change the types from the source domain into the equivalent types in the target domain, for 
example, changing String type usages in the UML model into the varchar type usages in the SQL model.

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

So for Standard and Professional edition users, transformations are not available.</p><p>Plugins can bring additional transformations regardless of the edition. For example, users, who have the Cameo Data Modeler plugin, can use the transformation engine to run ER to SQL(Generic/ Oracle) transformations, even if they do not have the Architect/Enterprise editions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Model Transformation Wizard enables running one of the predefined transformations. When using this wizard to run a chosen transformation, you have to perform the following steps:</p><ol><li>Choose a transformation type.</li><li>Specify both the transformation source model (or a part of it) and destination package.</li><li>Select a type map.</li><li>Set custom transformation properties.</li></ol><p>Each transformation converts the chosen model part into another model according to the rules of this transformation. Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains, e.g., UML and SQL.</p><p>All transformations follow a similar approach. They take a part of a model as the transformation source and copy it to the destination model, establishing traces between the transformation source and target elements. Then each transformation performs the specific model reorganizations, which are necessary for each <br />transformation type according to the transformation options specified by the user in the transformation wizard. Transformation can also be performed in-place, i.e., the source model is not copied to the destination model, but transformation works directly on it instead.</p><p>Transformations also perform the so-called type remapping. During the transformation between the different modeling domains, such as UML and SQL, it is necessary to go through the data types used in the source model and change the types from the source domain into the equivalent types in the target domain, for <br />example, changing String type usages in the UML model into the varchar type usages in the SQL model.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f7885535-c51b-4829-ad5b-3fbe2e6cd6f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995757 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01203: (2026x Refresh1) Model validation tool

- page_id: `303995757`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995757/2026x+Refresh1+Model+validation+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:59.810+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Model Validation tool allows templates to:

- Invoke the Validation menu and validate the models from suite names
- Set the severity.
- Retrieve validation results.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Model Validation dialog.

Like other Custom tools, the Model Validation tool '*validationtool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom tools and the installation, see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom tool' space='']*.

To import a Model Validation tool to a template, type this code in the template.

```text

```

Class ModelValidationTool

Methods:

- +validate( suiteName : String ) : List<ValidationResult>
- +validate( suiteName : String, severity: String ) : List<ValidationResult>
- +validate( suiteName : String, element : List, severity : String) : List<ValidationResult>

Class ValidationResult

Methods:

- +getAbbreviation() : String

#### Validate Methods

**validate(suiteName : String) : List<ValidationResult>**

Uses only a suite name to validate a model from the whole project with default “debug” as severity.

```text

```

**validate(suiteName : String, severity : String) : List<ValidationResult>**

Uses a suite name and a severity type to validate a model from the whole project. The severity types are “debug”, “*info*”, “*warning*”, “*error*” and “*fatal*”.

```text

```

**validate(suiteName : String, elementList : List<Element>, severity : String) : List<ValidationResult>**

Uses a suite name and a severity type to validate a model from an element list. The severity types are “*debug*”, “*info*”, “*warning*”, “*error*”, and “*fatal*”.

```text

```

To validate all classes by using ‘UML completeness constraints’ suite and the severity type is warning, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

**validate(suiteName : String, elementList : List<Element>, severity : String, excludeReadOnly : Boolean) : List<ValidationResult>**

Same as above, except last parameter allows to override environment option "Exclude elements from projects used in read-only mode", which is true by default.

```text

```

#### Getting the Suite Name List

The template author can list the entire available Model Validation suite names from ‘suiteNames’ methods. The ‘suiteNames’ is available in the ModelValidationtool class.

**getSuiteNames() : List<String>**

```text

```

**$validator** represents the ModelValidationtool class.

Prints all Metrics names and their values of all Classes, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

#### Getting the Validation Data from Validation Results

The object returned from validate methods in the previous section is the list of validation results. The validation results contain columns of validation result with each row of validated models.

**getElement() : Element**

Gets a validated element.

```text

```

To print all validated elements' names, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

**getSeverity() : String**

Gets a severity type.

```text

```

**getAbbreviation() : String**

Gets an abbreviation.

```text

```

**getMessage() : String**

Gets a message.

```text

```

**isIgnored() : Boolean**

Gets an isIgnored value to check whether the validation error has been ignored or not.

```text

```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995617 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01205: (2026x Refresh1) Model Visualizer

- page_id: `303995617`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995617/2026x+Refresh1+Model+Visualizer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:53.842+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

555902574

555902576

555902575

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Model Visualizer** helps to create elements from existing data and analyze the relationships between elements created in a UML model. It is also possible to analyze the inheritance and dependency relationships between classes.

All model visualizing and analyzing tools are presented as Wizards, with several steps that should be followed in order to accomplish the desired results.

All Wizards have the following common buttons:

- Add – add selected model elements from the All list to the Selected list.
- Add All – add all elements located in the same hierarchy level as the selected element to the Selected list.
- Add Recursively – add all elements in the selected packages and all elements from nested packages to the Selected list.
- Remove – remove the selected element from the Selected list.
- Remove All – remove all selected elements.
- Back – return to the previous dialog.
- Next – proceed to the next step.
- Finish – finish the configuration. All other options will be set by default. The Wizard exits and results are displayed.
- Cancel – cancel the wizard.
- Reset To Defaults – if changes were made to the element properties, values will be set to default.

555902573

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84622345-7942-4578-b5de-68782ee809d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">555902574</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d2d2447-e995-4eb3-93a4-cb724f24b30d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">555902576</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eebeeba6-8ed5-4b9f-a851-bfbbecab80de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">555902575</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4044c731-4b77-49a3-8f79-231d0123645a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <strong>Model Visualizer</strong> helps to create elements from existing data and analyze the relationships between elements created in a UML model. It is also possible to analyze the inheritance and dependency relationships between classes.</p><p>All model visualizing and analyzing tools are presented as Wizards, with several steps that should be followed in order to accomplish the desired results.</p><p>All Wizards have the following common buttons:</p><ul><li><strong>Add</strong> – add selected model elements from the <strong>All</strong> list to the <strong>Selected</strong> list.</li><li><strong>Add All</strong> – add all elements located in the same hierarchy level as the selected element to the <strong>Selected</strong> list.</li><li><strong>Add Recursively</strong> – add all elements in the selected packages and all elements from nested packages to the <strong>Selected</strong> list.</li><li><strong>Remove</strong> – remove the selected element from the <strong>Selected</strong> list.</li><li><strong>Remove All</strong> – remove all selected elements.</li><li><strong>Back</strong> – return to the previous dialog.</li><li><strong>Next</strong> – proceed to the next step.</li><li><strong>Finish</strong> – finish the configuration. All other options will be set by default. The <strong>Wizard</strong> exits and results are displayed. </li><li><strong>Cancel</strong> – cancel the wizard. </li><li><strong>Reset To Defaults</strong> – if changes were made to the element properties, values will be set to default.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af2f976b-8280-4498-89d0-c911f29fde6b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">555902573</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0b7701a7-74d6-4e2c-b22b-1aba43ae616f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991940 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01206: (2026x Refresh1) Modelica Bus

- page_id: `303991940`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991940/2026x+Refresh1+Modelica+Bus
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:21.263+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Modelica export > (2026x Refresh1) Advanced Modelica features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Connectors define how components interact and exchange signals. Modelica provides support for expandable and non-expandable connectors. Expandable connectors are ideal for modeling buses in complex systems as they support flexible and scalable connections without predefined structures. The **ModelicaBus stereotype** lets you export Interface Blocks as expandable connectors in Modelica, optionally including Proxy Ports. By default, ports are exported as simple connectors; applying the stereotype exports them as expandable connectors, preserving flexibility and scalability. To learn more about applying Stereotype, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **ModelicaBus**Steretype applied to an Interface Block.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Connectors define how components interact and exchange signals. Modelica provides support for expandable and non-expandable connectors. Expandable connectors are ideal for modeling buses in complex systems as they support flexible and scalable connections without predefined structures. The <strong>ModelicaBus stereotype</strong> lets you export Interface Blocks as expandable connectors in Modelica, optionally including Proxy Ports. By default, ports are exported as simple connectors; applying the stereotype exports them as expandable connectors, preserving flexibility and scalability. To learn more about applying Stereotype, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Modelica Bus Stereotype.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modelica Bus" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong>ModelicaBus </strong>Steretype applied to an Interface Block.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991710 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 01207: (2026x Refresh1) Modelica export

- page_id: `303991710`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991710/2026x+Refresh1+Modelica+export
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:15.412+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Export to Modelica tool allows quick and automatic synchronization between high-level system architecture and behaviour model definitions and Modelica simulation models by translating and mapping corresponding concepts and names. The Modelica export is based on SysPhS standard that is described in details in the[OMG SysPhS specification](https://www.omg.org/spec/SysPhS/About-SysPhS/)(SysML Extension for Physical Interaction and Signal Flow Simulation).You can use the Modelica export functionality in our tool to export your model to the Modelica file. The export includes blocks,parametrics, state machines, internal structures with ports, interfaces and connectors. The generated *.mo file is ready to open with Modelica tools. As example of exported model to the Modelica file, see the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *ConnectedTanks* Block exported to the Modelica file.

##### Preparing SysML v1 model for Modelica export

No special prerequisites are required to be able to use SysML v1 to Modelica transformation. Any existing pure SysML v1 models can be exported into Modelica with a high level of success. That is especially true for black-box architecture definitions (blocks, parts, ports, parameters, interfaces and connectors). However, if user wants to export full behavior implementation and expects it to be ready and simulatable in Modelica, some special annotation may be required. Modelica can only solve the models where number of variables is no more than number of equations. That means all SysML v1 properties can’t be exported as variables, some must be marked as constants or parameters.

**SysML v1 properties**

You can prepare your model by:

- Applying « PhSConstant » stereotype to a SysML v1 property if it has fixed value and not going to change during simulation. It is exported as Modelica parameter.
- Applying « PhSVariable » stereotype if you want to mark property as Modelica variable and fill appropriate tags (isContinuous, isConserved and changeCycle) if needed. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using SysPhs constant and SysPhs variable' space='']>]]>

If SysML v1 properties are not annotated with«PhSConstant»or«PhSVariable»stereotypes, they all are exported equally, as you select in the **Modelica Export Options** dialog: all as parameters, variables or skipped. >]]>

**Diagrams**

SysML IBD diagrams can be optionally converted into Modelica graphical annotations. Only one IBD diagram per Block is exported, so if you have more, make sure that the right one is the first under the Block in the model browser. Rename, remove or change the order if needed. >]]>

##### Exporting model to the Modelica file

You can export to the Modelica file:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] - exports the whole model (Read the procedure below).
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] - exports particular configuration with initial values and subtype instances as specified in the instance model. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instance Specifications export to the Modelica file' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] - partial export of parts, ports and connectors from this diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Partial model export to the Modelica file' space='']>]]>

To export the whole model to the Modelica file

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] , select the Block you want to export and do one of the following:
  - In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick File > Export To > Modelica .
  - Right-click the selected Block and then click Tools > Export to Modelica .
2. Specify export options in the Modelica Export Options dialog. Option descriptions >>
3. Click OK . The Modelica (.mo) file is generated and saved to your file directory.

###### Modelica Export Options

Each time you export your model to the Modelica file, the **Modelica Export Options** dialog opens. The detailed descriptions are provided in the following table.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Export not annotated SysML v1 properties as: | Select one of the following to define how the mechanism must export not annotated properties (read the definition below):as Variablesas ParametersDo not export Annotated SysML v1 properties definitionAn annotated SysML v1 properties are used in the model if the «PhsVariable» or «PhsConstant» stereotypes are applied for the Block properties. The detail information about «PhsVariable» or «PhsConstant» stereotypes, are provided in the Using SysPhs variable and SysPhs constant page and OMG SysPhS specification.Contrary to annotated SysML v1 properties, not annotated SysML v1 properties means that your model is without «PhsVariable» or «PhsConstant» stereotypes.You can check, if those stereotypes are used in your model by clicking in the and selecting the Show Applied Stereotypes option. The stereotypes becomes visible next to the element name in the . |
| Generate diagram layout annotations | If selected, converts SysML IBD diagram into Modelica graphical annotations. Only first IBD diagram of the Block is exported. If you have more, make sure the right one is the first under the Block in the Model Browser. Rename, remove or change the order if needed.You can modify the default value of the Generate Diagram layout annotations option in the project options dialog. To learn more about modifying the project options, refer to . |
|  | Click to specify the location of generated file or rename the file. |

**Related pages**

- Partial model export to the Modelica file
- Nested properties export to the Modelica file
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Extending models with Comments' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Modelica export is based on SysPhS standard that is described in details in the </span><a class="external-link" style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://www.omg.org/spec/SysPhS/About-SysPhS/" rel="nofollow">OMG SysPhS specification</a><span style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (SysML Extension for Physical Interaction and Signal Flow Simulation). </span><span style="font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can use the Modelica export functionality in our tool to export your model to the Modelica file. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992527 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01208: (2026x Refresh1) Modelica import

- page_id: `303992527`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992527/2026x+Refresh1+Modelica+import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:01.415+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Importing External Simulation Models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### **What is Modelica?**

TheModelica Languageis a non-proprietary, object-oriented, equation based language to conveniently model complex physical systems containing, for example, mechanical, electrical, electronic, hydraulic, thermal, control, electric power or process-oriented subcomponents.

##### **Modelica file import**

The modeling tools support**MO******file format, meaning that you must have your Modelica model in this format to import it. Following the Modelica file import, the **Modelica Import Options** dialog opens. It allows selecting features you want to update from the Modelica file, create, delete, or change the context-specific initial values. Blocks or Interface Blocks, introduced as property types, are created under the same owner as an imported context. The primitive type, if it was set, is also created for a Block or Interface Block. You can also create properties, connectors, and ports. Note that the imported Connectors are identified by the connector ends.

To import the Modelica file into the model

1. Select a Block.
2. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click File > Import From > Modelica File . [ATTACHMENT filename='modelica_import_menu.png']
3. Select the Modelica (.mo) file on your file system and click Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='modelica_import_select_file.png']
4. In the Modelica Import Options dialog, specify which features you want to update from the Modelica file.
5. Click Import .

###### An example of Modelica Import

The original Modelica data file for the example model:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The changes made in the Modelica data file are shown in the Modelica Import Options dialog, where you can choose whether to accept those changes or not:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><strong>What is Modelica?</strong></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The </span>Modelica Language<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> is a non-proprietary, object-oriented, equation based language to conveniently model complex physical systems containing, for example, mechanical, electrical, electronic, hydraulic, thermal, control, electric power or process-oriented subcomponents.</span></p><h3><strong>Modelica file import</strong></h3><p>The modeling tools support<strong> MO</strong><strong> </strong>file format, meaning that you must have your Modelica model in this format to import it. Following the Modelica file import, the <strong>Modelica Import Options</strong> dialog opens. It allows s<span>electing features you want to update from the Modelica file, create, delete, or change the context-specific initial values. Blocks or Interface Blocks, introduced as property types, are created under the same owner as an imported context. The primitive type, if it was set, is also created for a Block or Interface Block.  You can also create properties, connectors, and ports. Note that the imported Connectors are identified by the connector ends.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To import the Modelica file into the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Block.</li><li>In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Import From </strong>&gt; <strong>Modelica File</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_import_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modelica import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select the Modelica (.mo) file on your file system and click <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_import_select_file.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modelica import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Modelica Import Options</strong> dialog, specify which features you want to update from the Modelica file. </li><li>Click <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ol><h4>An example of Modelica Import</h4><p>The original Modelica data file for the example model:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_modelica_import.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modelica import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The changes made in the Modelica data file are shown in the Modelica Import Options dialog, where you can choose whether to accept those changes or not:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_import_options_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modelica import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991640 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 01209: (2026x Refresh1) Modeling functional flows with Activities

- page_id: `303991640`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991640/2026x+Refresh1+Modeling+functional+flows+with+Activities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:56.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Functional analysis
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A new SysML Activity Diagram should be created for every function defined in use the cases of a system you are modeling. Though there are two or even more swimlane partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the block which captures your system.Modeling the action flow stimulates the identification of logical subsystems of the whole system.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>A new SysML Activity Diagram should be created for every function defined in use the cases of a system you are modeling</span><span>. Though there are two or even more swimlane partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the block which captures your system</span><span>. </span><span>Modeling the action flow stimulates the identification of logical subsystems of the whole system</span><span>.</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a0c6177d-fe04-4c6f-b85e-7a0d22315935" /></p>
````

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82a0b04b-b852-46f5-9f79-f29bf7dd2287"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1403925404</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ff657d8-814f-4ba4-8462-469e77cedb69"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1403925406</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="824f771c-a977-4720-b8c0-e5f7c258ffa3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1403925405</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fb5b28f-206f-406f-a771-3c86b37ccf8e"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce151008-a2cb-4e73-981f-ff8565d86591"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1403925402</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c9dedc59-f81b-49e9-8fa4-a94c687eade1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
